1	/**
     2	 * @file javascript_types.def
     3	 * @brief JavaScript language node type mappings for AST semantic extraction
     4	 *
     5	 * This file defines the mapping from tree-sitter JavaScript node types to semantic
     6	 * categories, extraction strategies, and behavioral flags. JavaScript is the base
     7	 * for TypeScript, which extends these definitions via `#include`.
     8	 *
     9	 * @details
    10	 * Each entry uses the DEF_TYPE macro with the following signature:
    11	 * ```cpp
    12	 * DEF_TYPE(raw_type, semantic_type, name_extraction, native_extraction, flags)
    13	 * ```
    14	 *
    15	 * | Parameter | Description |
    16	 * |-----------|-------------|
    17	 * | raw_type | The tree-sitter node type string (e.g., `"function_declaration"`) |
    18	 * | semantic_type | Base semantic category, optionally OR'd with refinement bits |
    19	 * | name_extraction | Strategy enum from `ExtractionStrategy` for extracting node names |
    20	 * | native_extraction | Strategy enum from `NativeExtractionStrategy` for rich context |
    21	 * | flags | Bitwise OR of `ASTNodeFlags` values |
    22	 *
    23	 * ## JavaScript Language Characteristics
    24	 *
    25	 * - **Dynamic typing**: No static type annotations (see TypeScript for typed variant)
    26	 * - **First-class functions**: Functions are values, can be passed and returned
    27	 * - **Prototypal inheritance**: Objects inherit directly from other objects
    28	 * - **ES6+ classes**: Syntactic sugar over prototype-based inheritance
    29	 * - **Arrow functions**: Concise syntax with lexical `this` binding
    30	 * - **Async/await**: Promise-based asynchronous programming
    31	 * - **Destructuring**: Pattern matching for arrays and objects
    32	 * - **Template literals**: String interpolation with backticks
    33	 * - **Modules**: ES6 import/export system
    34	 * - **Spread/rest operators**: `...` for expansion and collection
    35	 *
    36	 * ## Semantic Type Encoding
    37	 *
    38	 * Semantic types use 8-bit encoding:
    39	 * - Bits 7-2: Base category (e.g., `DEFINITION_FUNCTION = 0x04`)
    40	 * - Bits 1-0: Refinement within category
    41	 *
    42	 * Example: `DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::LAMBDA`
    43	 *   - Base: 0x04 (function definition)
    44	 *   - Refinement: 0x01 (lambda/arrow)
    45	 *   - Combined: 0x05
    46	 *
    47	 * @see semantic_types.hpp for complete semantic type definitions
    48	 * @see node_config.hpp for extraction strategy enums and refinements
    49	 * @see typescript_types.def for TypeScript extensions
    50	 */
    51	
    52	// ============================================================================
    53	// FUNCTION DEFINITIONS
    54	// ============================================================================
    55	
    56	/**
    57	 * @defgroup javascript_functions Function Definitions
    58	 * @brief JavaScript function declaration forms
    59	 *
    60	 * JavaScript has multiple function syntaxes, each with different `this` binding
    61	 * and hoisting behavior. All JavaScript functions have bodies (IS_EMBODIED).
    62	 * @{
    63	 */
    64	
    65	/**
    66	 * @brief Named function declaration: `function name(params) { ... }`
    67	 * Hoisted to top of scope; creates named binding
    68	 */
    69	DEF_TYPE("function_declaration", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, FUNCTION_WITH_PARAMS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
    70	
    71	/**
    72	 * @brief Arrow function: `(params) => expr` or `(params) => { ... }`
    73	 * Lexically binds `this`; uses LAMBDA refinement
    74	 */
    75	DEF_TYPE("arrow_function", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::LAMBDA, FIND_ASSIGNMENT_TARGET, ARROW_FUNCTION, ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
    76	
    77	/**
    78	 * @brief Function expression: `const f = function(params) { ... }`
    79	 * Anonymous or named; not hoisted
    80	 */
    81	DEF_TYPE("function_expression", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::LAMBDA, FIND_ASSIGNMENT_TARGET, FUNCTION_WITH_PARAMS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
    82	
    83	/**
    84	 * @brief Method definition in class or object: `methodName(params) { ... }`
    85	 * Shorthand method syntax
    86	 */
    87	DEF_TYPE("method_definition", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, FUNCTION_WITH_PARAMS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
    88	
    89	/**
    90	 * @brief Async function: `async function name(params) { ... }`
    91	 * Returns Promise; can use await inside
    92	 */
    93	DEF_TYPE("async_function_declaration", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::ASYNC, FIND_IDENTIFIER, ASYNC_FUNCTION, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
    94	
    95	/**
    96	 * @brief Generator function expression: `function*(params) { ... }`
    97	 * Anonymous generator assigned to variable; can yield values
    98	 */
    99	DEF_TYPE("generator_function", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::ASYNC, FIND_ASSIGNMENT_TARGET, FUNCTION_WITH_PARAMS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
   100	
   101	/**
   102	 * @brief Generator function declaration: `function* name(params) { ... }`
   103	 * Named generator function at statement level
   104	 */
   105	DEF_TYPE("generator_function_declaration", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::ASYNC, FIND_IDENTIFIER, FUNCTION_WITH_PARAMS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
   106	
   107	/**
   108	 * @brief Async generator expression: `async function*(params) { ... }`

{"result":"     1	/**
     2	 * @file typescript_types.def
     3	 * @brief TypeScript language node type mappings for AST semantic extraction
     4	 *
     5	 * This file defines the mapping from tree-sitter TypeScript node types to semantic
     6	 * categories, extraction strategies, and behavioral flags. It extends JavaScript
     7	 * with static typing features.
     8	 *
     9	 * @details
    10	 * Each entry uses the DEF_TYPE macro with the following signature:
    11	 * ```cpp
    12	 * DEF_TYPE(raw_type, semantic_type, name_extraction, native_extraction, flags)
    13	 * ```
    14	 *
    15	 * | Parameter | Description |
    16	 * |-----------|-------------|
    17	 * | raw_type | The tree-sitter node type string (e.g., `"interface_declaration"`) |
    18	 * | semantic_type | Base semantic category, optionally OR'd with refinement bits |
    19	 * | name_extraction | Strategy enum from `ExtractionStrategy` for extracting node names |
    20	 * | native_extraction | Strategy enum from `NativeExtractionStrategy` for rich context |
    21	 * | flags | Bitwise OR of `ASTNodeFlags` values |
    22	 *
    23	 * ## TypeScript Language Characteristics
    24	 *
    25	 * - **Superset of JavaScript**: Includes all JS constructs via `#include`
    26	 * - **Static typing**: Compile-time type checking with structural typing
    27	 * - **Interfaces**: Contract definitions for object shapes (ABSTRACT refinement)
    28	 * - **Type aliases**: Named types via `type` keyword
    29	 * - **Enums**: Enumerated types (ENUM refinement)
    30	 * - **Generics**: Type parameters with constraints
    31	 * - **Access modifiers**: `public`, `private`, `protected`, `readonly`
    32	 * - **Decorators**: Metadata annotations via `@decorator` syntax
    33	 * - **Declaration files**: `.d.ts` ambient declarations
    34	 * - **Namespaces/modules**: Code organization constructs
    35	 *
    36	 * ## Semantic Type Encoding
    37	 *
    38	 * Semantic types use 8-bit encoding:
    39	 * - Bits 7-2: Base category (e.g., `DEFINITION_CLASS = 0x0C`)
    40	 * - Bits 1-0: Refinement within category
    41	 *
    42	 * Example: `DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::ABSTRACT`
    43	 *   - Base: 0x0C (class definition)
    44	 *   - Refinement: 0x01 (abstract/interface)
    45	 *   - Combined: 0x0D
    46	 *
    47	 * @see semantic_types.hpp for complete semantic type definitions
    48	 * @see node_config.hpp for extraction strategy enums and refinements
    49	 * @see javascript_types.def for inherited JavaScript definitions
    50	 */
    51	
    52	/**
    53	 * @brief Include JavaScript base definitions
    54	 *
    55	 * TypeScript is a superset of JavaScript, so we inherit all JavaScript
    56	 * node type mappings. TypeScript-specific constructs are defined below
    57	 * and may override JavaScript definitions where the TypeScript parser
    58	 * produces different node types.
    59	 */
    60	#include "javascript_types.def"
    61	
    62	// ============================================================================

    66	/**
    67	 * @defgroup typescript_type_system Type System Constructs
    68	 * @brief TypeScript's static type system definitions
    69	 *
    70	 * TypeScript adds a rich type system on top of JavaScript. These nodes
    71	 * represent type declarations, interfaces, and type signatures that
    72	 * exist only at compile time and are erased during transpilation.
    73	 * @{
    74	 */
    75	
    76	/**
    77	 * @brief Type alias declaration: `type Name = SomeType`
    78	 * Creates a named alias for a type expression
    79	 */
    80	DEF_TYPE("type_alias_declaration", DEFINITION_CLASS, FIND_IDENTIFIER, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, 0)
    81	
    82	/**
    83	 * @brief Interface declaration: `interface Name { ... }`
    84	 * Defines a structural type contract; uses ABSTRACT refinement
    85	 */
    86	DEF_TYPE("interface_declaration", DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::ABSTRACT, FIND_IDENTIFIER, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
    87	
    88	/**
    89	 * @brief Property signature in interface: `name: Type;`
    90	 * Declares a property that implementing objects must have
    91	 */
    92	DEF_TYPE("property_signature", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::FIELD, FIND_IDENTIFIER, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_DECLARATION_ONLY)
    93	
    94	/**
    95	 * @brief Method signature in interface: `methodName(params): ReturnType;`
    96	 * Declares a method that implementing objects must have
    97	 */
    98	DEF_TYPE("method_signature", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, FUNCTION_WITH_PARAMS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_DECLARATION_ONLY)
    99	
   100	/**
   101	 * @brief Construct signature: `new (params): Type`
   102	 * Defines how to construct instances; CONSTRUCTOR refinement
   103	 */
   104	DEF_TYPE("construct_signature", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::CONSTRUCTOR, NONE, CONSTRUCTOR_DEFINITION, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_DECLARATION_ONLY)
   105	
   106	/**
   107	 * @brief Call signature: `(params): ReturnType`
   108	 * Defines callable object signature
   109	 */
   110	DEF_TYPE("call_signature", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR, NONE, FUNCTION_WITH_PARAMS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_DECLARATION_ONLY)
   111	
   112	/**
   113	 * @brief Index signature: `[key: KeyType]: ValueType`
   114	 * Defines indexable object behavior
   115	 */
   116	DEF_TYPE("index_signature", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::FIELD, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_DECLARATION_ONLY)
   117	
   118	/**
   119	 * @brief Function signature (declaration only): `function name(params): Type;`
   120	 * Overload signatures or ambient declarations
   121	 */
   122	DEF_TYPE("function_signature", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, FUNCTION_WITH_PARAMS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_DECLARATION_ONLY)
   123	
   124	/** @} */ // end typescript_type_system

   770

   773

{"result":"     1	/**
     2	 * @file typescript_types.def
     3	 * @brief TypeScript language node type mappings for AST semantic extraction
     4	 *
     5	 * This file defines the mapping from tree-sitter TypeScript node types to semantic
     6	 * categories, extraction strategies, and behavioral flags. It extends JavaScript
     7	 * with static typing features.
     8	 *
     9	 * @details
    10	 * Each entry uses the DEF_TYPE macro with the following signature:
    11	 * ```cpp
    12	 * DEF_TYPE(raw_type, semantic_type, name_extraction, native_extraction, flags)
    13	 * ```
    14	 *
    15	 * | Parameter | Description |
    16	 * |-----------|-------------|
    17	 * | raw_type | The tree-sitter node type string (e.g., `"interface_declaration"`) |
    18	 * | semantic_type | Base semantic category, optionally OR'd with refinement bits |
    19	 * | name_extraction | Strategy enum from `ExtractionStrategy` for extracting node names |
    20	 * | native_extraction | Strategy enum from `NativeExtractionStrategy` for rich context |
    21	 * | flags | Bitwise OR of `ASTNodeFlags` values |
    22	 *
    23	 * ## TypeScript Language Characteristics
    24	 *
    25	 * - **Superset of JavaScript**: Includes all JS constructs via `#include`
    26	 * - **Static typing**: Compile-time type checking with structural typing
    27	 * - **Interfaces**: Contract definitions for object shapes (ABSTRACT refinement)
    28	 * - **Type aliases**: Named types via `type` keyword
    29	 * - **Enums**: Enumerated types (ENUM refinement)
    30	 * - **Generics**: Type parameters with constraints
    31	 * - **Access modifiers**: `public`, `private`, `protected`, `readonly`
    32	 * - **Decorators**: Metadata annotations via `@decorator` syntax
    33	 * - **Declaration files**: `.d.ts` ambient declarations
    34	 * - **Namespaces/modules**: Code organization constructs
    35	 *
    36	 * ## Semantic Type Encoding
    37	 *
    38	 * Semantic types use 8-bit encoding:
    39	 * - Bits 7-2: Base category (e.g., `DEFINITION_CLASS = 0x0C`)
    40	 * - Bits 1-0: Refinement within category
    41	 *
    42	 * Example: `DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::ABSTRACT`
    43	 *   - Base: 0x0C (class definition)
    44	 *   - Refinement: 0x01 (abstract/interface)
    45	 *   - Combined: 0x0D
    46	 *
    47	 * @see semantic_types.hpp for complete semantic type definitions
    48	 * @see node_config.hpp for extraction strategy enums and refinements
    49	 * @see javascript_types.def for inherited JavaScript definitions
    50	 */
    51	
    52	/**
    53	 * @brief Include JavaScript base definitions
    54	 *
    55	 * TypeScript is a superset of JavaScript, so we inherit all JavaScript
    56	 * node type mappings. TypeScript-specific constructs are defined below
    57	 * and may override JavaScript definitions where the TypeScript parser
    58	 * produces different node types.
    59	 */
    60	#include "javascript_types.def"
    61	
    62	// ============================================================================
    63	// TYPESCRIPT-SPECIFIC CONSTRUCTS - Based on ~1000 file analysis
    64	// ============================================================================
    65	
    66	/**
    67	 * @defgroup typescript_type_system Type System Constructs
    68	 * @brief TypeScript's static type system definitions
    69	 *
    70	 * TypeScript adds a rich type system on top of JavaScript. These nodes
    71	 * represent type declarations, interfaces, and type signatures that
    72	 * exist only at compile time and are erased during transpilation.
    73	 * @{
    74	 */
    75	
    76	/**
    77	 * @brief Type alias declaration: `type Name = SomeType`
    78	 * Creates a named alias for a type expression
    79	 */
    80	DEF_TYPE("type_alias_declaration", DEFINITION_CLASS, FIND_IDENTIFIER, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, 0)
    81	
    82	/**
    83	 * @brief Interface declaration: `interface Name { ... }`
    84	 * Defines a structural type contract; uses ABSTRACT refinement
    85	 */
    86	DEF_TYPE("interface_declaration", DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::ABSTRACT, FIND_IDENTIFIER, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
    87	
    88	/**
    89	 * @brief Property signature in interface: `name: Type;`
    90	 * Declares a property that implementing objects must have
    91	 */
    92	DEF_TYPE("property_signature", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::FIELD, FIND_IDENTIFIER, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_DECLARATION_ONLY)
    93	
    94	/**
    95	 * @brief Method signature in interface: `methodName(params): ReturnType;`
    96	 * Declares a method that implementing objects must have
    97	 */
    98	DEF_TYPE("method_signature", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, FUNCTION_WITH_PARAMS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_DECLARATION_ONLY)
    99	
   100	/**
   101	 * @brief Construct signature: `new (params): Type`
   102	 * Defines how to construct instances; CONSTRUCTOR refinement
   103	 */
   104	DEF_TYPE("construct_signature", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::CONSTRUCTOR, NONE, CONSTRUCTOR_DEFINITION, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_DECLARATION_ONLY)
   105	
   106	/**
   107	 * @brief Call signature: `(params): ReturnType`
   108	 * Defines callable object signature
   109	 */
   110	DEF_TYPE("call_signature", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR, NONE, FUNCTION_WITH_PARAMS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_DECLARATION_ONLY)
   111	
   112	/**
   113	 * @brief Index signature: `[key: KeyType]: ValueType`
   114	 * Defines indexable object behavior
   115	 */
   116	DEF_TYPE("index_signature", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::FIELD, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_DECLARATION_ONLY)
   117	
   118	/**
   119	 * @brief Function signature (declaration only): `function name(params): Type;`
   120	 * Overload signatures or ambient declarations
   121	 */
   122	DEF_TYPE("function_signature", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, FUNCTION_WITH_PARAMS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_DECLARATION_ONLY)
   123	
   124	/** @} */ // end typescript_type_system
   125	
   126	/**
   127	 * @defgroup typescript_type_annotations Type Annotations and References
   128	 * @brief Type annotations attached to declarations
   129	 *
   130	 * These nodes represent type annotations (`: Type`), type identifiers,
   131	 * and predefined primitive types.
   132	 * @{
   133	 */
   134	
   135	/** @brief Type annotation syntax: `: Type` */
   136	DEF_TYPE("type_annotation", TYPE_REFERENCE, NONE, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, 0)
   137	
   138	/** @brief Type identifier reference: `MyType` */
   139	DEF_TYPE("type_identifier", TYPE_REFERENCE, NODE_TEXT, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, 0)
   140	
   141	/** @brief Predefined/primitive type: `string`, `number`, `boolean`, etc. */
   142	DEF_TYPE("predefined_type", TYPE_PRIMITIVE, NODE_TEXT, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, 0)
   143	
   144	/** @brief This type: `this` in return type position */
   145	DEF_TYPE("this_type", TYPE_REFERENCE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   146	
   147	/** @brief Nested type identifier: `Namespace.Type` */
   148	DEF_TYPE("nested_type_identifier", TYPE_REFERENCE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   149	
   150	/** @} */ // end typescript_type_annotations
   151	
   152	/**
   153	 * @defgroup typescript_composite_types Composite Types
   154	 * @brief Union, intersection, array, tuple, and other composite types
   155	 *
   156	 * TypeScript's type combinators allow building complex types from simpler ones.
   157	 * @{
   158	 */
   159	
   160	/** @brief Union type: `Type1 | Type2 | Type3` */
   161	DEF_TYPE("union_type", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   162	
   163	/** @brief Intersection type: `Type1 & Type2` */
   164	DEF_TYPE("intersection_type", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   165	
   166	/** @brief Array type: `Type[]` */
   167	DEF_TYPE("array_type", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   168	
   169	/** @brief Tuple type: `[Type1, Type2, ...]` */
   170	DEF_TYPE("tuple_type", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   171	
   172	/** @brief Object type literal: `{ prop: Type; ... }` */
   173	DEF_TYPE("object_type", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   174	
   175	/** @brief Function type: `(params) => ReturnType` */
   176	DEF_TYPE("function_type", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   177	
   178	/** @brief Constructor type: `new (params) => Type` */
   179	DEF_TYPE("constructor_type", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   180	
   181	/** @brief Template literal type: `` `prefix${Type}suffix` `` */
   182	DEF_TYPE("template_literal_type", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   183	
   184	/** @brief Template type (generic instantiation) */
   185	DEF_TYPE("template_type", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   186	
   187	/** @} */ // end typescript_composite_types
   188	
   189	/**
   190	 * @defgroup typescript_literal_types Literal Types
   191	 * @brief Types representing specific literal values
   192	 *
   193	 * TypeScript allows literal values as types for precise type narrowing.
   194	 * @{
   195	 */
   196	
   197	/** @brief Generic literal type */
   198	DEF_TYPE("literal_type", TYPE_PRIMITIVE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   199	
   200	/** @brief String literal type: `"specific-string"` */
   201	DEF_TYPE("string_literal_type", TYPE_PRIMITIVE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   202	
   203	/** @brief Number literal type: `42` as a type */
   204	DEF_TYPE("number_literal_type", TYPE_PRIMITIVE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   205	
   206	/** @brief Boolean literal type: `true` or `false` as a type */
   207	DEF_TYPE("boolean_literal_type", TYPE_PRIMITIVE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   208	
   209	/** @} */ // end typescript_literal_types
   210	
   211	/**
   212	 * @defgroup typescript_generics Generic/Template Types
   213	 * @brief Parameterized types and type parameters
   214	 *
   215	 * TypeScript supports generics with constraints and defaults.
   216	 * @{
   217	 */
   218	
   219	/** @brief Type parameters list: `<T, U extends Base>` */
   220	DEF_TYPE("type_parameters", TYPE_GENERIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   221	
   222	/** @brief Single type parameter: `T` or `T extends Base` */
   223	DEF_TYPE("type_parameter", TYPE_GENERIC, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   224	
   225	/** @brief Type arguments list: `<string, number>` */
   226	DEF_TYPE("type_arguments", TYPE_GENERIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   227	
   228	/** @brief Generic type instantiation: `Array<T>` */
   229	DEF_TYPE("generic_type", TYPE_GENERIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   230	
   231	/** @brief Type constraint: `extends SomeType` */
   232	DEF_TYPE("constraint", TYPE_REFERENCE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   233	
   234	/** @brief Default type: `= DefaultType` */
   235	DEF_TYPE("default_type", TYPE_REFERENCE, NONE, NONE, 0)

1	/**
     2	 * @file typescript_types.def
     3	 * @brief TypeScript language node type mappings for AST semantic extraction
     4	 *
     5	 * This file defines the mapping from tree-sitter TypeScript node types to semantic
     6	 * categories, extraction strategies, and behavioral flags. It extends JavaScript
     7	 * with static typing features.
     8	 *
     9	 * @details
    10	 * Each entry uses the DEF_TYPE macro with the following signature:
    11	 * ```cpp
    12	 * DEF_TYPE(raw_type, semantic_type, name_extraction, native_extraction, flags)
    13	 * ```
    14	 *
    15	 * | Parameter | Description |
    16	 * |-----------|-------------|
    17	 * | raw_type | The tree-sitter node type string (e.g., `"interface_declaration"`) |
    18	 * | semantic_type | Base semantic category, optionally OR'd with refinement bits |
    19	 * | name_extraction | Strategy enum from `ExtractionStrategy` for extracting node names |
    20	 * | native_extraction | Strategy enum from `NativeExtractionStrategy` for rich context |
    21	 * | flags | Bitwise OR of `ASTNodeFlags` values |
    22	 *
    23	 * ## TypeScript Language Characteristics
    24	 *
    25	 * - **Superset of JavaScript**: Includes all JS constructs via `#include`
    26	 * - **Static typing**: Compile-time type checking with structural typing
    27	 * - **Interfaces**: Contract definitions for object shapes (ABSTRACT refinement)
    28	 * - **Type aliases**: Named types via `type` keyword
    29	 * - **Enums**: Enumerated types (ENUM refinement)
    30	 * - **Generics**: Type parameters with constraints
    31	 * - **Access modifiers**: `public`, `private`, `protected`, `readonly`
    32	 * - **Decorators**: Metadata annotations via `@decorator` syntax
    33	 * - **Declaration files**: `.d.ts` ambient declarations
    34	 * - **Namespaces/modules**: Code organization constructs
    35	 *
    36	 * ## Semantic Type Encoding
    37	 *
    38	 * Semantic types use 8-bit encoding:
    39	 * - Bits 7-2: Base category (e.g., `DEFINITION_CLASS = 0x0C`)
    40	 * - Bits 1-0: Refinement within category
    41	 *
    42	 * Example: `DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::ABSTRACT`
    43	 *   - Base: 0x0C (class definition)
    44	 *   - Refinement: 0x01 (abstract/interface)
    45	 *   - Combined: 0x0D
    46	 *
    47	 * @see semantic_types.hpp for complete semantic type definitions
    48	 * @see node_config.hpp for extraction strategy enums and refinements
    49	 * @see javascript_types.def for inherited JavaScript definitions
    50	 */
    51	
    52	/**
    53	 * @brief Include JavaScript base definitions
    54	 *
    55	 * TypeScript is a superset of JavaScript, so we inherit all JavaScript
    56	 * node type mappings. TypeScript-specific constructs are defined below
    57	 * and may override JavaScript definitions where the TypeScript parser
    58	 * produces different node types.
    59	 */
    60	#include "javascript_types.def"
    61	
    62	// ============================================================================
    63	// TYPESCRIPT-SPECIFIC CONSTRUCTS - Based on ~1000 file analysis
    64	// ============================================================================
    65	
    66	/**
    67	 * @defgroup typescript_type_system Type System Constructs
    68	 * @brief TypeScript's static type system definitions
    69	 *
    70	 * TypeScript adds a rich type system on top of JavaScript. These nodes
    71	 * represent type declarations, interfaces, and type signatures that
    72	 * exist only at compile time and are erased during transpilation.
    73	 * @{
    74	 */
    75	
    76	/**
    77	 * @brief Type alias declaration: `type Name = SomeType`
    78	 * Creates a named alias for a type expression
    79	 */
    80	DEF_TYPE("type_alias_declaration", DEFINITION_CLASS, FIND_IDENTIFIER, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, 0)
    81	
    82	/**
    83	 * @brief Interface declaration: `interface Name { ... }`
    84	 * Defines a structural type contract; uses ABSTRACT refinement
    85	 */
    86	DEF_TYPE("interface_declaration", DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::ABSTRACT, FIND_IDENTIFIER, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
    87	
    88	/**
    89	 * @brief Property signature in interface: `name: Type;`
    90	 * Declares a property that implementing objects must have
    91	 */
    92	DEF_TYPE("property_signature", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::FIELD, FIND_IDENTIFIER, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_DECLARATION_ONLY)
    93	
    94	/**
    95	 * @brief Method signature in interface: `methodName(params): ReturnType;`
    96	 * Declares a method that implementing objects must have
    97	 */
    98	DEF_TYPE("method_signature", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, FUNCTION_WITH_PARAMS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_DECLARATION_ONLY)
    99	
   100	/**
   101	 * @brief Construct signature: `new (params): Type`
   102	 * Defines how to construct instances; CONSTRUCTOR refinement
   103	 */
   104	DEF_TYPE("construct_signature", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::CONSTRUCTOR, NONE, CONSTRUCTOR_DEFINITION, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_DECLARATION_ONLY)
   105	
   106	/**
   107	 * @brief Call signature: `(params): ReturnType`
   108	 * Defines callable object signature
   109	 */
   110	DEF_TYPE("call_signature", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR, NONE, FUNCTION_WITH_PARAMS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_DECLARATION_ONLY)
   111	
   112	/**
   113	 * @brief Index signature: `[key: KeyType]: ValueType`
   114	 * Defines indexable object behavior
   115	 */
   116	DEF_TYPE("index_signature", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::FIELD, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_DECLARATION_ONLY)
   117	
   118	/**
   119	 * @brief Function signature (declaration only): `function name(params): Type;`
   120	 * Overload signatures or ambient declarations
   121	 */
   122	DEF_TYPE("function_signature", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, FUNCTION_WITH_PARAMS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_DECLARATION_ONLY)
   123	
   124	/** @} */ // end typescript_type_system
   125	
   126	/**
   127	 * @defgroup typescript_type_annotations Type Annotations and References

   133	 */
   134	
   135	/** @brief Type annotation syntax: `: Type` */
   136	DEF_TYPE("type_annotation", TYPE_REFERENCE, NONE, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, 0)

   139	DEF_TYPE("type_identifier", TYPE_REFERENCE, NODE_TEXT, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, 0)
   140	
   141	/** @brief Predefined/primitive type: `string`, `number`, `boolean`, etc. */
   142	DEF_TYPE("predefined_type", TYPE_PRIMITIVE, NODE_TEXT, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, 0)
   143	
   144	/** @brief This type: `this` in return type position */
   145	DEF_TYPE("this_type", TYPE_REFERENCE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   146	
   147	/** @brief Nested type identifier: `Namespace.Type` */
   148	DEF_TYPE("nested_type_identifier", TYPE_REFERENCE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   149	
   150	/** @} */ // end typescript_type_annotations
   151	
   152	/**
   153	 * @defgroup typescript_composite_types Composite Types
   154	 * @brief Union, intersection, array, tuple, and other composite types
   155	 *
   156	 * TypeScript's type combinators allow building complex types from simpler ones.
   157	 * @{
   158	 */
   159	
   160	/** @brief Union type: `Type1 | Type2 | Type3` */
   161	DEF_TYPE("union_type", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   162	
   163	/** @brief Intersection type: `Type1 & Type2` */
   164	DEF_TYPE("intersection_type", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   165	
   166	/** @brief Array type: `Type[]` */
   167	DEF_TYPE("array_type", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   168	
   169	/** @brief Tuple type: `[Type1, Type2, ...]` */
   170	DEF_TYPE("tuple_type", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   171	
   172	/** @brief Object type literal: `{ prop: Type; ... }` */
   173	DEF_TYPE("object_type", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   174	
   175	/** @brief Function type: `(params) => ReturnType` */
   176	DEF_TYPE("function_type", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   177	
   178	/** @brief Constructor type: `new (params) => Type` */
   179	DEF_TYPE("constructor_type", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   180	
   181	/** @brief Template literal type: `` `prefix${Type}suffix` `` */
   182	DEF_TYPE("template_literal_type", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   183	
   184	/** @brief Template type (generic instantiation) */
   185	DEF_TYPE("template_type", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   186	
   187	/** @} */ // end typescript_composite_types
   188	
   189	/**
   190	 * @defgroup typescript_literal_types Literal Types
   191	 * @brief Types representing specific literal values
   192	 *
   193	 * TypeScript allows literal values as types for precise type narrowing.
   194	 * @{
   195	 */
   196	
   197	/** @brief Generic literal type */
   198	DEF_TYPE("literal_type", TYPE_PRIMITIVE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   199	
   200	/** @brief String literal type: `"specific-string"` */
   201	DEF_TYPE("string_literal_type", TYPE_PRIMITIVE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   202	
   203	/** @brief Number literal type: `42` as a type */
   204	DEF_TYPE("number_literal_type", TYPE_PRIMITIVE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   205	
   206	/** @brief Boolean literal type: `true` or `false` as a type */
   207	DEF_TYPE("boolean_literal_type", TYPE_PRIMITIVE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   208	
   209	/** @} */ // end typescript_literal_types
   210	
   211	/**
   212	 * @defgroup typescript_generics Generic/Template Types
   213	 * @brief Parameterized types and type parameters
   214	 *
   215	 * TypeScript supports generics with constraints and defaults.
   216	 * @{
   217	 */
   218	
   219	/** @brief Type parameters list: `<T, U extends Base>` */
   220	DEF_TYPE("type_parameters", TYPE_GENERIC, NONE, NONE, 0)

   224	
   225	/** @brief Type arguments list: `<string, number>` */
   226	DEF_TYPE("type_arguments", TYPE_GENERIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   227

   229	DEF_TYPE("generic_type", TYPE_GENERIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   230	
   231	/** @brief Type constraint: `extends SomeType` */
   232	DEF_TYPE("constraint", TYPE_REFERENCE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   233	
   234	/** @brief Default type: `= DefaultType` */
   235	DEF_TYPE("default_type", TYPE_REFERENCE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   236	
   237	/** @} */ // end typescript_generics

   246

   248	DEF_TYPE("conditional_type", TYPE_REFERENCE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   249

content.thinking:
Both JavaScript and TypeScript are consistent for the core punctuation:
- `(`, `)`, `[`, `]`, `{`, `}` → `PARSER_DELIMITER`
- `,`, `;`, `:`, `.` → `PARSER_PUNCTUATION`

TypeScript also adds `<` and `>` as `PARSER_DELIMITER` (for generic type brackets), which is TypeScript-specific.

Now let me read the remaining def files. I'll batch them together to save time.

{"result":"     1	/**
     2	 * @file java_types.def
     3	 * @brief Java language node type mappings for AST semantic extraction
     4	 *
     5	 * @details This file defines how tree-sitter Java AST nodes are mapped to
     6	 * universal semantic types, along with strategies for extracting names and
     7	 * native context from the parse tree.
     8	 *
     9	 * ## DEF_TYPE Macro Format
    10	 *
    11	 * ```cpp
    12	 * DEF_TYPE(raw_type, semantic_type, name_extraction, native_extraction, flags)
    13	 * ```
    14	 *
    15	 * @param raw_type The tree-sitter node type string (e.g., "class_declaration")
    16	 * @param semantic_type Universal semantic classification with optional refinement bits
    17	 * @param name_extraction Strategy for extracting the node's name (see ExtractionStrategy)
    18	 * @param native_extraction Strategy for rich context extraction (see NativeExtractionStrategy)
    19	 * @param flags Behavioral flags (e.g., IS_KEYWORD, IS_SYNTAX_ONLY)
    20	 *
    21	 * ## Semantic Type Encoding
    22	 *
    23	 * Semantic types are 8-bit values where:
    24	 * - Bits 7-2: Base semantic category (e.g., DEFINITION_FUNCTION = 0x04)
    25	 * - Bits 1-0: Refinement within category (e.g., Function::LAMBDA = 0x01)
    26	 *
    27	 * Combined using bitwise OR:
    28	 * ```cpp
    29	 * DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::LAMBDA  // = 0x05
    30	 * ```
    31	 *
    32	 * ## Java Language Characteristics
    33	 *
    34	 * Java has several unique features that affect AST mapping:
    35	 *
    36	 * - **Class-based OOP**: Everything exists within classes; no standalone functions
    37	 * - **Package system**: Hierarchical namespace via packages
    38	 * - **Access modifiers**: `public`, `private`, `protected`, package-private (default)
    39	 * - **Static members**: Class-level methods and fields
    40	 * - **Final keyword**: Immutable variables, prevent method override, sealed classes
    41	 * - **Constructors**: Special methods for object initialization (CONSTRUCTOR refinement)
    42	 * - **Initializer blocks**: Static and instance initialization blocks
    43	 * - **Interfaces**: Abstract types, can have default methods (Java 8+)
    44	 * - **Enums**: Type-safe enumerations with associated behavior
    45	 * - **Annotations**: Metadata via `@Annotation` syntax
    46	 * - **Generics**: Type parameters on classes and methods
    47	 * - **Lambda expressions**: Functional interfaces (Java 8+)
    48	 * - **Method references**: `::` operator for method handles
    49	 * - **Records**: Immutable data classes (Java 16+)
    50	 * - **Checked exceptions**: Exception handling is part of method signatures
    51	 * - **Enhanced for**: Iterator-based for-each loops
    52	 * - **Switch expressions**: Pattern matching in switch (Java 14+)
    53	 *
    54	 * @see node_config.hpp for ExtractionStrategy and NativeExtractionStrategy definitions

    63	 * @defgroup java_packages Packages and Imports

    65	 *
    66	 * Java organizes code into packages. The `program` node represents the entire
    67	 * compilation unit (source file). Import statements bring in classes from

    80	/**
    81	 * @defgroup java_classes Class and Type Definitions
    82	 * @brief Classes, interfaces, enums, annotations, and records
    83	 *
    84	 * Java's type system includes:
    85	 * - `class`: Regular classes (REGULAR refinement)
    86	 * - `interface`: Abstract types (ABSTRACT refinement)
    87	 * - `enum`: Type-safe enumerations (ENUM refinement)
    88	 * - `annotation_type`: Custom annotation definitions (ABSTRACT refinement)
    89	 * - `record`: Immutable data carriers (Java 16+)
    90	 *
    91	 * All Java type definitions have bodies and are marked with IS_EMBODIED.
    92	 * @{
    93	 */
    94	DEF_TYPE("class_declaration", DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
    95	DEF_TYPE("interface_declaration", DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::ABSTRACT, FIND_IDENTIFIER, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
    96	DEF_TYPE("enum_declaration", DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::ENUM, FIND_IDENTIFIER, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
    97	DEF_TYPE("annotation_type_declaration", DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::ABSTRACT, FIND_IDENTIFIER, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
    98	DEF_TYPE("record_declaration", DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
    99	/** @} */

   102	// METHOD AND CONSTRUCTOR DEFINITIONS

   104	
   105	/**
   106	 * @defgroup java_methods Method and Constructor Definitions
   107	 * @brief Methods, constructors, and initializer blocks
   108	 *
   109	 * Java methods are always inside classes. Constructors are special methods
   110	 * that initialize objects and get the CONSTRUCTOR refinement.
   111	 *
   112	 * Initializer blocks:
   113	 * - `static_initializer`: Runs once when class is loaded
   114	 * - `instance_initializer`: Runs each time an object is created
   115	 *
   116	 * Lambda expressions (Java 8+) are anonymous functions implementing
   117	 * functional interfaces.
   118	 * @{
   119	 */
   120	DEF_TYPE("method_declaration", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, FUNCTION_WITH_PARAMS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
   121	DEF_TYPE("constructor_declaration", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::CONSTRUCTOR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, CONSTRUCTOR_DEFINITION, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
   122	DEF_TYPE("static_initializer", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
   123	DEF_TYPE("instance_initializer", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
   124	DEF_TYPE("lambda_expression", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::LAMBDA, FIND_ASSIGNMENT_TARGET, ARROW_FUNCTION, ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
   125	/** @} */

   131	/**
   132	 * @defgroup java_variables Field and Variable Declarations
   133	 * @brief Fields, local variables, and parameters
   134	 *
   135	 * Java variables are mutable by default. The `final` modifier makes them
   136	 * immutable, but this is handled via modifier nodes rather than refinements.
   137	 *
   138	 * Variable types:
   139	 * - `field_declaration`: Class-level fields (FIELD refinement)
   140	 * - `local_variable_declaration`: Method-local variables (MUTABLE refinement)
   141	 * - `formal_parameter`: Method parameters (PARAMETER refinement)
   142	 * - `enum_constant`: Enum values (FIELD refinement)
   143	 * @{
   144	 */
   145	DEF_TYPE("field_declaration", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::FIELD, FIND_IN_DECLARATOR, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, 0)
   146	DEF_TYPE("local_variable_declaration", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::MUTABLE, FIND_IN_DECLARATOR, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, 0)
   147	DEF_TYPE("variable_declarator", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::MUTABLE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, 0)
   148	DEF_TYPE("formal_parameter", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::PARAMETER, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   149	DEF_TYPE("spread_parameter", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::PARAMETER, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   150	DEF_TYPE("catch_formal_parameter", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::PARAMETER, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   151	DEF_TYPE("enum_constant", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::FIELD, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   152	/** @} */

   181	/**
   182	 * @defgroup java_type_system Type System
   183	 * @brief Type references, primitives, generics, and arrays
   184	 *
   185	 * Java has both primitive types and reference types:
   186	 * - Primitives: `int`, `boolean`, `float`, etc. (TYPE_PRIMITIVE)
   187	 * - Reference types: Classes, interfaces, arrays (TYPE_REFERENCE)
   188	 * - Generics: Type parameters `<T>` (TYPE_GENERIC)
   189	 * - Arrays: `Type[]` (TYPE_COMPOSITE)

   191	 * The `void` keyword is also treated as a type for return type declarations.
   192	 * @{
   193	 */

   216	/**
   217	 * @defgroup java_operators Operators and Expressions
   218	 * @brief Arithmetic, logical, comparison, and assignment operators
   219	 *
   220	 * Java operators include:
   221	 * - Arithmetic: `+`, `-`, `*`, `/`, `%`, bitwise, shifts
   222	 * - Logical: `&&`, `||`, `!`
   223	 * - Comparison: `==`, `!=`, `<`, `>`, `<=`, `>=`, `instanceof`
   224	 * - Assignment: `=`, `+=`, `-=`, etc.
   225	 * - Ternary: `? :`
   226	 * - Update: `++`, `--` (prefix and postfix)

   228	 * Java has unsigned right shift `>>>` unlike C/C++.
   229	 * @{
   230	 */

   291	/**
   292	 * @defgroup java_control Control Flow
   293	 * @brief Conditionals, loops, and jump statements
   294	 *
   295	 * Java control flow constructs:
   296	 * - `if`/`else`: Binary conditional
   297	 * - `switch`: Multiway branching (statements and expressions)
   298	 * - `while`/`do-while`: Condition-based loops
   299	 * - `for`: Traditional counter-based loop
   300	 * - `enhanced_for`: Iterator-based for-each loop
   301	 *
   302	 * Loop refinements:
   303	 * - COUNTER: Traditional `for(int i=0; i<n; i++)`
   304	 * - ITERATOR: Enhanced for `for(Type x : collection)`
   305	 * - CONDITIONAL: `while` and `do-while` loops
   306	 * @{
   307	 */
   308	
   309	// Conditional statements
   310	DEF_TYPE("if_statement", FLOW_CONDITIONAL | SemanticRefinements::Conditional::BINARY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   311	DEF_TYPE("switch_statement", FLOW_CONDITIONAL | SemanticRefinements::Conditional::MULTIWAY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   312	DEF_TYPE("switch_expression", FLOW_CONDITIONAL | SemanticRefinements::Conditional::MULTIWAY, NONE, NONE, 0)

   324	DEF_TYPE("yield_statement", FLOW_JUMP | SemanticRefinements::Jump::RETURN, NONE, NONE, 0)
   325	/** @} */

   504	DEF_TYPE(">>>=", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   505	DEF_TYPE("->", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   506	
   507	// Comparison operators
   508	DEF_TYPE("==", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   509	DEF_TYPE("!=", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   510	DEF_TYPE("<", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   511	DEF_TYPE(">", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   512	DEF_TYPE("<=", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   513	DEF_TYPE(">=", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   514	
   515	// Arithmetic operators
   516	DEF_TYPE("+", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   517	DEF_TYPE("-", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   518	DEF_TYPE("*", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   519	DEF_TYPE("/", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   520	DEF_TYPE("%", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   521	DEF_TYPE("&", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   522	DEF_TYPE("|", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   523	DEF_TYPE("^", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   524	DEF_TYPE("~", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   525	DEF_TYPE("<<", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   526	DEF_TYPE(">>", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   527	DEF_TYPE(">>>", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   528	DEF_TYPE("++", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   529	DEF_TYPE("--", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   530	
   531	// Logical operators
   532	DEF_TYPE("&&", OPERATOR_LOGICAL, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   533	DEF_TYPE("||", OPERATOR_LOGICAL, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   534	DEF_TYPE("!", OPERATOR_LOGICAL, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   535	
   536	// Other operators
   537	DEF_TYPE("?", FLOW_CONDITIONAL, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   538	DEF_TYPE("::", COMPUTATION_ACCESS, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   539	/** @} */
   540	
   541	// =============================================================================
   542	// PUNCTUATION AND DELIMITERS
   543	// =============================================================================
   544	
   545	/**
   546	 * @defgroup java_punctuation Punctuation
   547	 * @brief Delimiters, separators, and syntax markers
   548	 * @{
   549	 */
   550	DEF_TYPE("(", PARSER_DELIMITER, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   551	DEF_TYPE(")", PARSER_DELIMITER, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   552	DEF_TYPE("[", PARSER_DELIMITER, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   553	DEF_TYPE("]", PARSER_DELIMITER, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   554	DEF_TYPE("{", PARSER_DELIMITER, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   555	DEF_TYPE("}", PARSER_DELIMITER, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   556	DEF_TYPE("\"", PARSER_DELIMITER, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   557	DEF_TYPE("'", PARSER_DELIMITER, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   558	DEF_TYPE(",", PARSER_PUNCTUATION, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   559	DEF_TYPE(";", PARSER_PUNCTUATION, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   560	DEF_TYPE(".", PARSER_PUNCTUATION, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   561	DEF_TYPE(":", PARSER_PUNCTUATION, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   562	/** @} */
"}

{"result":"     1	/**
     2	 * @file java_types.def
     3	 * @brief Java language node type mappings for AST semantic extraction
     4	 *
     5	 * @details This file defines how tree-sitter Java AST nodes are mapped to
     6	 * universal semantic types, along with strategies for extracting names and
     7	 * native context from the parse tree.
     8	 *
     9	 * ## DEF_TYPE Macro Format
    10	 *
    11	 * ```cpp
    12	 * DEF_TYPE(raw_type, semantic_type, name_extraction, native_extraction, flags)
    13	 * ```
    14	 *
    15	 * @param raw_type The tree-sitter node type string (e.g., "class_declaration")
    16	 * @param semantic_type Universal semantic classification with optional refinement bits
    17	 * @param name_extraction Strategy for extracting the node's name (see ExtractionStrategy)
    18	 * @param native_extraction Strategy for rich context extraction (see NativeExtractionStrategy)
    19	 * @param flags Behavioral flags (e.g., IS_KEYWORD, IS_SYNTAX_ONLY)
    20	 *
    21	 * ## Semantic Type Encoding
    22	 *
    23	 * Semantic types are 8-bit values where:
    24	 * - Bits 7-2: Base semantic category (e.g., DEFINITION_FUNCTION = 0x04)
    25	 * - Bits 1-0: Refinement within category (e.g., Function::LAMBDA = 0x01)
    26	 *
    27	 * Combined using bitwise OR:
    28	 * ```cpp
    29	 * DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::LAMBDA  // = 0x05
    30	 * ```
    31	 *
    32	 * ## Java Language Characteristics
    33	 *
    34	 * Java has several unique features that affect AST mapping:
    35	 *
    36	 * - **Class-based OOP**: Everything exists within classes; no standalone functions
    37	 * - **Package system**: Hierarchical namespace via packages
    38	 * - **Access modifiers**: `public`, `private`, `protected`, package-private (default)
    39	 * - **Static members**: Class-level methods and fields
    40	 * - **Final keyword**: Immutable variables, prevent method override, sealed classes
    41	 * - **Constructors**: Special methods for object initialization (CONSTRUCTOR refinement)
    42	 * - **Initializer blocks**: Static and instance initialization blocks
    43	 * - **Interfaces**: Abstract types, can have default methods (Java 8+)
    44	 * - **Enums**: Type-safe enumerations with associated behavior
    45	 * - **Annotations**: Metadata via `@Annotation` syntax
    46	 * - **Generics**: Type parameters on classes and methods
    47	 * - **Lambda expressions**: Functional interfaces (Java 8+)
    48	 * - **Method references**: `::` operator for method handles
    49	 * - **Records**: Immutable data classes (Java 16+)
    50	 * - **Checked exceptions**: Exception handling is part of method signatures
    51	 * - **Enhanced for**: Iterator-based for-each loops
    52	 * - **Switch expressions**: Pattern matching in switch (Java 14+)
    53	 *
    54	 * @see node_config.hpp for ExtractionStrategy and NativeExtractionStrategy definitions
    55	 * @see semantic_types.hpp for full semantic type enumeration
    56	 */
    57	
    58	// =============================================================================
    59	// COMPILATION UNIT AND PACKAGES
    60	// =============================================================================
    61	
    62	/**
    63	 * @defgroup java_packages Packages and Imports
    64	 * @brief Package declarations and import statements
    65	 *
    66	 * Java organizes code into packages. The `program` node represents the entire
    67	 * compilation unit (source file). Import statements bring in classes from
    68	 * other packages.
    69	 * @{
    70	 */
    71	DEF_TYPE("program", DEFINITION_MODULE, NONE, NONE, 0)
    72	DEF_TYPE("package_declaration", DEFINITION_MODULE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
    73	DEF_TYPE("import_declaration", EXTERNAL_IMPORT | SemanticRefinements::Import::MODULE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
    74	/** @} */
    75	
    76	// =============================================================================
    77	// CLASS AND TYPE DEFINITIONS
    78	// =============================================================================
    79	
    80	/**
    81	 * @defgroup java_classes Class and Type Definitions
    82	 * @brief Classes, interfaces, enums, annotations, and records
    83	 *
    84	 * Java's type system includes:
    85	 * - `class`: Regular classes (REGULAR refinement)
    86	 * - `interface`: Abstract types (ABSTRACT refinement)
    87	 * - `enum`: Type-safe enumerations (ENUM refinement)
    88	 * - `annotation_type`: Custom annotation definitions (ABSTRACT refinement)
    89	 * - `record`: Immutable data carriers (Java 16+)
    90	 *
    91	 * All Java type definitions have bodies and are marked with IS_EMBODIED.
    92	 * @{
    93	 */
    94	DEF_TYPE("class_declaration", DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
    95	DEF_TYPE("interface_declaration", DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::ABSTRACT, FIND_IDENTIFIER, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
    96	DEF_TYPE("enum_declaration", DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::ENUM, FIND_IDENTIFIER, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
    97	DEF_TYPE("annotation_type_declaration", DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::ABSTRACT, FIND_IDENTIFIER, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
    98	DEF_TYPE("record_declaration", DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
    99	/** @} */
   100	
   101	// =============================================================================
   102	// METHOD AND CONSTRUCTOR DEFINITIONS
   103	// =============================================================================
   104	
   105	/**
   106	 * @defgroup java_methods Method and Constructor Definitions
   107	 * @brief Methods, constructors, and initializer blocks
   108	 *
   109	 * Java methods are always inside classes. Constructors are special methods
   110	 * that initialize objects and get the CONSTRUCTOR refinement.
   111	 *
   112	 * Initializer blocks:
   113	 * - `static_initializer`: Runs once when class is loaded
   114	 * - `instance_initializer`: Runs each time an object is created
   115	 *
   116	 * Lambda expressions (Java 8+) are anonymous functions implementing
   117	 * functional interfaces.
   118	 * @{
   119	 */
   120	DEF_TYPE("method_declaration", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, FUNCTION_WITH_PARAMS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
   121	DEF_TYPE("constructor_declaration", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::CONSTRUCTOR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, CONSTRUCTOR_DEFINITION, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
   122	DEF_TYPE("static_initializer", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
   123	DEF_TYPE("instance_initializer", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
   124	DEF_TYPE("lambda_expression", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::LAMBDA, FIND_ASSIGNMENT_TARGET, ARROW_FUNCTION, ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
   125	/** @} */
   126	
   127	// =============================================================================
   128	// FIELD AND VARIABLE DECLARATIONS
   129	// =============================================================================
   130	
   131	/**
   132	 * @defgroup java_variables Field and Variable Declarations
   133	 * @brief Fields, local variables, and parameters
   134	 *
   135	 * Java variables are mutable by default. The `final` modifier makes them
   136	 * immutable, but this is handled via modifier nodes rather than refinements.
   137	 *
   138	 * Variable types:
   139	 * - `field_declaration`: Class-level fields (FIELD refinement)
   140	 * - `local_variable_declaration`: Method-local variables (MUTABLE refinement)
   141	 * - `formal_parameter`: Method parameters (PARAMETER refinement)
   142	 * - `enum_constant`: Enum values (FIELD refinement)
   143	 * @{
   144	 */
   145	DEF_TYPE("field_declaration", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::FIELD, FIND_IN_DECLARATOR, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, 0)
   146	DEF_TYPE("local_variable_declaration", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::MUTABLE, FIND_IN_DECLARATOR, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, 0)
   147	DEF_TYPE("variable_declarator", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::MUTABLE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, 0)
   148	DEF_TYPE("formal_parameter", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::PARAMETER, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   149	DEF_TYPE("spread_parameter", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::PARAMETER, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   150	DEF_TYPE("catch_formal_parameter", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::PARAMETER, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   151	DEF_TYPE("enum_constant", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::FIELD, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   152	/** @} */
   153	
   154	// =============================================================================
   155	// METHOD CALLS AND ACCESS
   156	// =============================================================================
   157	
   158	/**
   159	 * @defgroup java_calls Method Calls and Access
   160	 * @brief Method invocations, object creation, and field access
   161	 *
   162	 * Java call expressions:
   163	 * - `method_invocation`: Calling methods on objects (METHOD refinement)
   164	 * - `object_creation_expression`: `new` keyword (CONSTRUCTOR refinement)
   165	 * - `method_reference`: `Class::method` syntax (Java 8+)
   166	 *
   167	 * Field and array access are separate node types.
   168	 * @{
   169	 */
   170	DEF_TYPE("method_invocation", COMPUTATION_CALL | SemanticRefinements::Call::METHOD, FIND_CALL_TARGET, FUNCTION_CALL, 0)
   171	DEF_TYPE("object_creation_expression", COMPUTATION_CALL | SemanticRefinements::Call::CONSTRUCTOR, FIND_CALL_TARGET, FUNCTION_CALL, 0)
   172	DEF_TYPE("method_reference", COMPUTATION_ACCESS, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   173	DEF_TYPE("field_access", COMPUTATION_ACCESS, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   174	DEF_TYPE("array_access", COMPUTATION_ACCESS, NONE, NONE, 0)
   175	/** @} */
   176	
   177	// =============================================================================
   178	// TYPE SYSTEM
   179	// =============================================================================
   180	
   181	/**
   182	 * @defgroup java_type_system Type System
   183	 * @brief Type references, primitives, generics, and arrays
   184	 *
   185	 * Java has both primitive types and reference types:
   186	 * - Primitives: `int`, `boolean`, `float`, etc. (TYPE_PRIMITIVE)
   187	 * - Reference types: Classes, interfaces, arrays (TYPE_REFERENCE)
   188	 * - Generics: Type parameters `<T>` (TYPE_GENERIC)
   189	 * - Arrays: `Type[]` (TYPE_COMPOSITE)
   190	 *
   191	 * The `void` keyword is also treated as a type for return type declarations.
   192	 * @{
   193	 */
   194	
   195	// Type references
   196	DEF_TYPE("type_identifier", TYPE_REFERENCE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   197	DEF_TYPE("scoped_type_identifier", TYPE_REFERENCE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   198	
   199	// Primitive types
   200	DEF_TYPE("integral_type", TYPE_PRIMITIVE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   201	DEF_TYPE("floating_point_type", TYPE_PRIMITIVE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   202	DEF_TYPE("boolean_type", TYPE_PRIMITIVE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   203	DEF_TYPE("void_type", TYPE_PRIMITIVE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   204	
   205	// Composite and generic types
   206	DEF_TYPE("array_type", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   207	DEF_TYPE("dimensions", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   208	DEF_TYPE("generic_type", TYPE_GENERIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   209	DEF_TYPE("type_arguments", TYPE_GENERIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   210	/** @} */
   211	
   212	// =============================================================================
   213	// OPERATORS AND EXPRESSIONS
   214	// =============================================================================
   215	
   216	/**
   217	 * @defgroup java_operators Operators and Expressions
   218	 * @brief Arithmetic, logical, comparison, and assignment operators
   219	 *
   220	 * Java operators include:
   221	 * - Arithmetic: `+`, `-`, `*`, `/`, `%`, bitwise, shifts
   222	 * - Logical: `&&`, `||`, `!`
   223	 * - Comparison: `==`, `!=`, `<`, `>`, `<=`, `>=`, `instanceof`
   224	 * - Assignment: `=`, `+=`, `-=`, etc.
   225	 * - Ternary: `? :`
   226	 * - Update: `++`, `--` (prefix and postfix)
   227	 *
   228	 * Java has unsigned right shift `>>>` unlike C/C++.
   229	 * @{
   230	 */
   231	DEF_TYPE("assignment_expression", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT | SemanticRefinements::Assignment::SIMPLE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   232	DEF_TYPE("binary_expression", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC | SemanticRefinements::Arithmetic::BINARY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   233	DEF_TYPE("unary_expression", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC | SemanticRefinements::Arithmetic::UNARY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   234	DEF_TYPE("update_expression", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC | SemanticRefinements::Arithmetic::UNARY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   235	DEF_TYPE("ternary_expression", FLOW_CONDITIONAL | SemanticRefinements::Conditional::TERNARY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   236	DEF_TYPE("parenthesized_expression", COMPUTATION_EXPRESSION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   237	/** @} */
   238	
   239	// =============================================================================
   240	// IDENTIFIERS AND LITERALS
   241	// =============================================================================
   242	
   243	/**
   244	 * @defgroup java_identifiers Identifiers
   245	 * @brief Identifier and scoped identifier nodes
   246	 * @{
   247	 */
   248	DEF_TYPE("identifier", NAME_IDENTIFIER, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   249	DEF_TYPE("scoped_identifier", NAME_QUALIFIED, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   250	/** @} */
   251	
   252	/**
   253	 * @defgroup java_literals Literal Values
   254	 * @brief Numeric, string, and boolean literals
   255	 *
   256	 * Java numeric literals can have various bases:
   257	 * - Decimal: `123`
   258	 * - Hexadecimal: `0xFF`
   259	 * - Octal: `0777`
   260	 * - Binary: `0b1010`
   261	 *
   262	 * String literals support escape sequences. Character literals use single quotes.
   263	 * @{
   264	 */
   265	
   266	// Integer literals (all bases)
   267	DEF_TYPE("decimal_integer_literal", LITERAL_NUMBER | SemanticRefinements::Number::INTEGER, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   268	DEF_TYPE("hex_integer_literal", LITERAL_NUMBER | SemanticRefinements::Number::INTEGER, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   269	DEF_TYPE("octal_integer_literal", LITERAL_NUMBER | SemanticRefinements::Number::INTEGER, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   270	DEF_TYPE("binary_integer_literal", LITERAL_NUMBER | SemanticRefinements::Number::INTEGER, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   271	
   272	// Floating point literals
   273	DEF_TYPE("decimal_floating_point_literal", LITERAL_NUMBER | SemanticRefinements::Number::FLOAT, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   274	DEF_TYPE("hex_floating_point_literal", LITERAL_NUMBER | SemanticRefinements::Number::FLOAT, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   275	
   276	// Boolean and null literals
   277	DEF_TYPE("true", LITERAL_ATOMIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   278	DEF_TYPE("false", LITERAL_ATOMIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   279	DEF_TYPE("null_literal", LITERAL_ATOMIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   280	
   281	// String and character literals
   282	DEF_TYPE("string_literal", LITERAL_STRING | SemanticRefinements::String::LITERAL, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   283	DEF_TYPE("string_fragment", LITERAL_STRING | SemanticRefinements::String::LITERAL, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   284	DEF_TYPE("character_literal", LITERAL_STRING | SemanticRefinements::String::LITERAL, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   285	/** @} */
   286	
   287	// =============================================================================
   288	// CONTROL FLOW
   289	// =============================================================================
   290	
   291	/**
   292	 * @defgroup java_control Control Flow
   293	 * @brief Conditionals, loops, and jump statements
   294	 *
   295	 * Java control flow constructs:
   296	 * - `if`/`else`: Binary conditional
   297	 * - `switch`: Multiway branching (statements and expressions)
   298	 * - `while`/`do-while`: Condition-based loops
   299	 * - `for`: Traditional counter-based loop
   300	 * - `enhanced_for`: Iterator-based for-each loop
   301	 *
   302	 * Loop refinements:
   303	 * - COUNTER: Traditional `for(int i=0; i<n; i++)`
   304	 * - ITERATOR: Enhanced for `for(Type x : collection)`
   305	 * - CONDITIONAL: `while` and `do-while` loops
   306	 * @{
   307	 */
   308	
   309	// Conditional statements
   310	DEF_TYPE("if_statement", FLOW_CONDITIONAL | SemanticRefinements::Conditional::BINARY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   311	DEF_TYPE("switch_statement", FLOW_CONDITIONAL | SemanticRefinements::Conditional::MULTIWAY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   312	DEF_TYPE("switch_expression", FLOW_CONDITIONAL | SemanticRefinements::Conditional::MULTIWAY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   313	
   314	// Loop statements
   315	DEF_TYPE("while_statement", FLOW_LOOP | SemanticRefinements::Loop::CONDITIONAL, NONE, NONE, 0)
   316	DEF_TYPE("do_statement", FLOW_LOOP | SemanticRefinements::Loop::CONDITIONAL, NONE, NONE, 0)
   317	DEF_TYPE("for_statement", FLOW_LOOP | SemanticRefinements::Loop::COUNTER, NONE, NONE, 0)
   318	DEF_TYPE("enhanced_for_statement", FLOW_LOOP | SemanticRefinements::Loop::ITERATOR, NONE, NONE, 0)
   319	
   320	// Jump statements
   321	DEF_TYPE("break_statement", FLOW_JUMP | SemanticRefinements::Jump::BREAK, NONE, NONE, 0)
   322	DEF_TYPE("continue_statement", FLOW_JUMP | SemanticRefinements::Jump::CONTINUE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   323	DEF_TYPE("return_statement", FLOW_JUMP | SemanticRefinements::Jump::RETURN, NONE, NONE, 0)
   324	DEF_TYPE("yield_statement", FLOW_JUMP | SemanticRefinements::Jump::RETURN, NONE, NONE, 0)
   325	/** @} */
   326	
   327	// =============================================================================
   328	// EXCEPTION HANDLING
   329	// =============================================================================
   330	
   331	/**
   332	 * @defgroup java_exceptions Exception Handling
   333	 * @brief Try/catch/finally and throw statements
   334	 *
   335	 * Java has checked exceptions that must be declared or caught. The
   336	 * `try_with_resources_statement` (Java 7+) automatically closes resources.
   337	 *
   338	 * Exception types:
   339	 * - ERROR_TRY: `try` and `try-with-resources` blocks
   340	 * - ERROR_CATCH: `catch` clauses
   341	 * - ERROR_FINALLY: `finally` blocks
   342	 * - ERROR_THROW: `throw` statements and `throws` declarations
   343	 * @{
   344	 */
   345	DEF_TYPE("try_statement", ERROR_TRY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   346	DEF_TYPE("try_with_resources_statement", ERROR_TRY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   347	DEF_TYPE("catch_clause", ERROR_CATCH, NONE, NONE, 0)
   348	DEF_TYPE("catch_type", ERROR_CATCH, NONE, NONE, 0)
   349	DEF_TYPE("finally_clause", ERROR_FINALLY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   350	DEF_TYPE("throw_statement", ERROR_THROW, NONE, NONE, 0)
   351	/** @} */
   352	
   353	// =============================================================================
   354	// ANNOTATIONS
   355	// =============================================================================
   356	
   357	/**
   358	 * @defgroup java_annotations Annotations
   359	 * @brief Java annotation syntax
   360	 *
   361	 * Annotations provide metadata for classes, methods, and fields.
   362	 * Common annotations: `@Override`, `@Deprecated`, `@SuppressWarnings`.
   363	 *
   364	 * - `marker_annotation`: Annotation without arguments (`@Override`)
   365	 * - `annotation`: Annotation with arguments (`@SuppressWarnings("unchecked")`)
   366	 * @{
   367	 */
   368	DEF_TYPE("annotation", METADATA_ANNOTATION, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   369	DEF_TYPE("marker_annotation", METADATA_ANNOTATION, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   370	DEF_TYPE("annotation_argument_list", ORGANIZATION_LIST, NONE, NONE, 0)
   371	DEF_TYPE("@", NAME_ATTRIBUTE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   372	/** @} */
   373	
   374	// =============================================================================
   375	// MODIFIERS
   376	// =============================================================================
   377	
   378	/**
   379	 * @defgroup java_modifiers Modifiers
   380	 * @brief Access modifiers and other modifier keywords
   381	 *
   382	 * Java modifiers control visibility and behavior:
   383	 * - Access: `public`, `private`, `protected` (default is package-private)
   384	 * - Other: `static`, `final`, `abstract`, `synchronized`, `volatile`, etc.
   385	 *
   386	 * Modifiers are children of declaration nodes and affect their semantics.
   387	 * @{
   388	 */
   389	DEF_TYPE("modifiers", METADATA_ANNOTATION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   390	DEF_TYPE("modifier", METADATA_ANNOTATION, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   391	/** @} */
   392	
   393	// =============================================================================
   394	// COMMENTS
   395	// =============================================================================
   396	
   397	/// @defgroup java_comments Comments
   398	/// @brief Line and block comments
   399	///
   400	/// Java has line comments and block comments.
   401	/// Javadoc comments are a special form of block comments.
   402	/// @{
   403	DEF_TYPE("line_comment", METADATA_COMMENT, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   404	DEF_TYPE("block_comment", METADATA_COMMENT, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   405	/** @} */
   406	
   407	// =============================================================================
   408	// STRUCTURAL ELEMENTS
   409	// =============================================================================
   410	
   411	/**
   412	 * @defgroup java_structure Structural Elements
   413	 * @brief Blocks, lists, and organizational nodes
   414	 * @{
   415	 */
   416	DEF_TYPE("block", ORGANIZATION_BLOCK | SemanticRefinements::Organization::SEQUENTIAL, NONE, NONE, 0)
   417	DEF_TYPE("class_body", ORGANIZATION_BLOCK, NONE, NONE, 0)
   418	DEF_TYPE("interface_body", ORGANIZATION_BLOCK, NONE, NONE, 0)
   419	DEF_TYPE("enum_body", ORGANIZATION_BLOCK, NONE, NONE, 0)
   420	DEF_TYPE("constructor_body", ORGANIZATION_BLOCK, NONE, NONE, 0)
   421	DEF_TYPE("argument_list", ORGANIZATION_LIST | SemanticRefinements::Organization::COLLECTION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   422	DEF_TYPE("formal_parameters", ORGANIZATION_LIST | SemanticRefinements::Organization::COLLECTION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   423	DEF_TYPE("expression_statement", EXECUTION_STATEMENT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   424	/** @} */
   425	
   426	// =============================================================================
   427	// KEYWORDS
   428	// =============================================================================
   429	
   430	/**
   431	 * @defgroup java_keywords Keywords
   432	 * @brief Java reserved words as syntax tokens
   433	 *
   434	 * Keywords are marked with IS_KEYWORD flag and get the same semantic type
   435	 * as the constructs they introduce. This enables semantic queries that include
   436	 * or exclude keyword tokens as needed.
   437	 * @{
   438	 */
   439	
   440	// Access modifier keywords
   441	DEF_TYPE("public", METADATA_ANNOTATION, NODE_TEXT, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   442	DEF_TYPE("private", METADATA_ANNOTATION, NODE_TEXT, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   443	DEF_TYPE("protected", METADATA_ANNOTATION, NODE_TEXT, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   444	
   445	// Other modifier keywords
   446	DEF_TYPE("static", METADATA_ANNOTATION, NODE_TEXT, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   447	DEF_TYPE("final", METADATA_ANNOTATION, NODE_TEXT, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   448	DEF_TYPE("abstract", METADATA_ANNOTATION, NODE_TEXT, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   449	
   450	// Type definition keywords
   451	DEF_TYPE("class", DEFINITION_CLASS, NODE_TEXT, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   452	DEF_TYPE("interface", DEFINITION_CLASS, NODE_TEXT, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   453	DEF_TYPE("enum", DEFINITION_CLASS, NODE_TEXT, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   454	DEF_TYPE("extends", TYPE_REFERENCE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   455	DEF_TYPE("implements", TYPE_REFERENCE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   456	
   457	// Package and import keywords
   458	DEF_TYPE("package", DEFINITION_MODULE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   459	DEF_TYPE("import", EXTERNAL_IMPORT, NODE_TEXT, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   460	
   461	// Control flow keywords
   462	DEF_TYPE("if", FLOW_CONDITIONAL, NODE_TEXT, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   463	DEF_TYPE("for", FLOW_LOOP, NODE_TEXT, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   464	DEF_TYPE("return", FLOW_JUMP, NODE_TEXT, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   465	
   466	// Exception keywords
   467	DEF_TYPE("try", ERROR_TRY, NODE_TEXT, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   468	DEF_TYPE("catch", ERROR_CATCH, NODE_TEXT, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   469	DEF_TYPE("throw", ERROR_THROW, NODE_TEXT, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   470	DEF_TYPE("throws", ERROR_THROW, NODE_TEXT, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   471	
   472	// Object keywords
   473	DEF_TYPE("new", COMPUTATION_CALL, NODE_TEXT, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   474	DEF_TYPE("this", NAME_SCOPED, NODE_TEXT, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   475	DEF_TYPE("super", NAME_SCOPED, NODE_TEXT, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   476	DEF_TYPE("instanceof", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NODE_TEXT, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   477	
   478	// Primitive type keywords
   479	DEF_TYPE("int", TYPE_PRIMITIVE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   480	/** @} */
   481	
   482	// =============================================================================
   483	// OPERATORS
   484	// =============================================================================
   485	
   486	/**
   487	 * @defgroup java_operators_tokens Operator Tokens
   488	 * @brief Individual operator symbols
   489	 * @{
   490	 */
   491	
   492	// Assignment operators
   493	DEF_TYPE("=", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   494	DEF_TYPE("+=", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   495	DEF_TYPE("-=", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   496	DEF_TYPE("*=", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   497	DEF_TYPE("/=", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   498	DEF_TYPE("%=", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   499	DEF_TYPE("&=", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   500	DEF_TYPE("|=", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   501	DEF_TYPE("^=", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   502	DEF_TYPE("<<=", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   503	DEF_TYPE(">>=", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   504	DEF_TYPE(">>>=", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   505	DEF_TYPE("->", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   506	
   507	// Comparison operators
   508	DEF_TYPE("==", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   509	DEF_TYPE("!=", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   510	DEF_TYPE("<", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   511	DEF_TYPE(">", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   512	DEF_TYPE("<=", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   513	DEF_TYPE(">=", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   514	
   515	// Arithmetic operators
   516	DEF_TYPE("+", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   517	DEF_TYPE("-", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   518	DEF_TYPE("*", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   519	DEF_TYPE("/", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   520	DEF_TYPE("%", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   521	DEF_TYPE("&", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   522	DEF_TYPE("|", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   523	DEF_TYPE("^", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   524	DEF_TYPE("~", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   525	DEF_TYPE("<<", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   526	DEF_TYPE(">>", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   527	DEF_TYPE(">>>", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   528	DEF_TYPE("++", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   529	DEF_TYPE("--", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   530	
   531	// Logical operators
   532	DEF_TYPE("&&", OPERATOR_LOGICAL, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   533	DEF_TYPE("||", OPERATOR_LOGICAL, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   534	DEF_TYPE("!", OPERATOR_LOGICAL, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   535	
   536	// Other operators
   537	DEF_TYPE("?", FLOW_CONDITIONAL, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   538	DEF_TYPE("::", COMPUTATION_ACCESS, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   539	/** @} */
   540	
   541	// =============================================================================
   542	// PUNCTUATION AND DELIMITERS
   543	// =============================================================================
   544	
   545	/**
   546	 * @defgroup java_punctuation Punctuation
   547	 * @brief Delimiters, separators, and syntax markers
   548	 * @{
   549	 */
   550	DEF_TYPE("(", PARSER_DELIMITER, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   551	DEF_TYPE(")", PARSER_DELIMITER, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   552	DEF_TYPE("[", PARSER_DELIMITER, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   553	DEF_TYPE("]", PARSER_DELIMITER, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   554	DEF_TYPE("{", PARSER_DELIMITER, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   555	DEF_TYPE("}", PARSER_DELIMITER, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   556	DEF_TYPE("\"", PARSER_DELIMITER, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   557	DEF_TYPE("'", PARSER_DELIMITER, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   558	DEF_TYPE(",", PARSER_PUNCTUATION, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   559	DEF_TYPE(";", PARSER_PUNCTUATION, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   560	DEF_TYPE(".", PARSER_PUNCTUATION, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   561	DEF_TYPE(":", PARSER_PUNCTUATION, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   562	/** @} */
"}
