1	/**
     2	 * @file java_types.def
     3	 * @brief Java language node type mappings for AST semantic extraction
     4	 *
     5	 * @details This file defines how tree-sitter Java AST nodes are mapped to
     6	 * universal semantic types, along with strategies for extracting names and
     7	 * native context from the parse tree.
     8	 *
     9	 * ## DEF_TYPE Macro Format
    10	 *
    11	 * ```cpp
    12	 * DEF_TYPE(raw_type, semantic_type, name_extraction, native_extraction, flags)
    13	 * ```
    14	 *
    15	 * @param raw_type The tree-sitter node type string (e.g., "class_declaration")
    16	 * @param semantic_type Universal semantic classification with optional refinement bits
    17	 * @param name_extraction Strategy for extracting the node's name (see ExtractionStrategy)
    18	 * @param native_extraction Strategy for rich context extraction (see NativeExtractionStrategy)
    19	 * @param flags Behavioral flags (e.g., IS_KEYWORD, IS_SYNTAX_ONLY)
    20	 *
    21	 * ## Semantic Type Encoding
    22	 *
    23	 * Semantic types are 8-bit values where:
    24	 * - Bits 7-2: Base semantic category (e.g., DEFINITION_FUNCTION = 0x04)
    25	 * - Bits 1-0: Refinement within category (e.g., Function::LAMBDA = 0x01)
    26	 *
    27	 * Combined using bitwise OR:
    28	 * ```cpp
    29	 * DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::LAMBDA  // = 0x05
    30	 * ```
    31	 *
    32	 * ## Java Language Characteristics
    33	 *
    34	 * Java has several unique features that affect AST mapping:
    35	 *
    36	 * - **Class-based OOP**: Everything exists within classes; no standalone functions
    37	 * - **Package system**: Hierarchical namespace via packages
    38	 * - **Access modifiers**: `public`, `private`, `protected`, package-private (default)
    39	 * - **Static members**: Class-level methods and fields
    40	 * - **Final keyword**: Immutable variables, prevent method override, sealed classes
    41	 * - **Constructors**: Special methods for object initialization (CONSTRUCTOR refinement)
    42	 * - **Initializer blocks**: Static and instance initialization blocks
    43	 * - **Interfaces**: Abstract types, can have default methods (Java 8+)
    44	 * - **Enums**: Type-safe enumerations with associated behavior
    45	 * - **Annotations**: Metadata via `@Annotation` syntax
    46	 * - **Generics**: Type parameters on classes and methods
    47	 * - **Lambda expressions**: Functional interfaces (Java 8+)
    48	 * - **Method references**: `::` operator for method handles
    49	 * - **Records**: Immutable data classes (Java 16+)
    50	 * - **Checked exceptions**: Exception handling is part of method signatures
    51	 * - **Enhanced for**: Iterator-based for-each loops
    52	 * - **Switch expressions**: Pattern matching in switch (Java 14+)
    53	 *
    54	 * @see node_config.hpp for ExtractionStrategy and NativeExtractionStrategy definitions
    55	 * @see semantic_types.hpp for full semantic type enumeration
    56	 */
    57	
    58	// =============================================================================
    59	// COMPILATION UNIT AND PACKAGES
    60	// =============================================================================
    61	
    62	/**
    63	 * @defgroup java_packages Packages and Imports
    64	 * @brief Package declarations and import statements
    65	 *
    66	 * Java organizes code into packages. The `program` node represents the entire
    67	 * compilation unit (source file). Import statements bring in classes from
    68	 * other packages.
    69	 * @{
    70	 */
    71	DEF_TYPE("program", DEFINITION_MODULE, NONE, NONE, 0)
    72	DEF_TYPE("package_declaration", DEFINITION_MODULE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
    73	DEF_TYPE("import_declaration", EXTERNAL_IMPORT | SemanticRefinements::Import::MODULE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
    74	/** @} */
    75	
    76	// =============================================================================
    77	// CLASS AND TYPE DEFINITIONS
    78	// =============================================================================
    79	
    80	/**
    81	 * @defgroup java_classes Class and Type Definitions
    82	 * @brief Classes, interfaces, enums, annotations, and records
    83	 *
    84	 * Java's type system includes:
    85	 * - `class`: Regular classes (REGULAR refinement)
    86	 * - `interface`: Abstract types (ABSTRACT refinement)
    87	 * - `enum`: Type-safe enumerations (ENUM refinement)
    88	 * - `annotation_type`: Custom annotation definitions (ABSTRACT refinement)
    89	 * - `record`: Immutable data carriers (Java 16+)
    90	 *
    91	 * All Java type definitions have bodies and are marked with IS_EMBODIED.
    92	 * @{
    93	 */
    94	DEF_TYPE("class_declaration", DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
    95	DEF_TYPE("interface_declaration", DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::ABSTRACT, FIND_IDENTIFIER, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
    96	DEF_TYPE("enum_declaration", DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::ENUM, FIND_IDENTIFIER, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
    97	DEF_TYPE("annotation_type_declaration", DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::ABSTRACT, FIND_IDENTIFIER, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
    98	DEF_TYPE("record_declaration", DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
    99	/** @} */
   100	
   101	// =============================================================================
   102	// METHOD AND CONSTRUCTOR DEFINITIONS
   103	// =============================================================================
   104	
   105	/**
   106	 * @defgroup java_methods Method and Constructor Definitions
   107	 * @brief Methods, constructors, and initializer blocks
   108	 *
   109	 * Java methods are always inside classes. Constructors are special methods
   110	 * that initialize objects and get the CONSTRUCTOR refinement.
   111	 *
   112	 * Initializer blocks:
   113	 * - `static_initializer`: Runs once when class is loaded
   114	 * - `instance_initializer`: Runs each time an object is created
   115	 *
   116	 * Lambda expressions (Java 8+) are anonymous functions implementing
   117	 * functional interfaces.
   118	 * @{
   119	 */
   120	DEF_TYPE("method_declaration", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, FUNCTION_WITH_PARAMS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
   121	DEF_TYPE("constructor_declaration", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::CONSTRUCTOR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, CONSTRUCTOR_DEFINITION, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
   122	DEF_TYPE("static_initializer", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
   123	DEF_TYPE("instance_initializer", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
   124	DEF_TYPE("lambda_expression", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::LAMBDA, FIND_ASSIGNMENT_TARGET, ARROW_FUNCTION, ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
   125	/** @} */
   126	
   127	// =============================================================================
   128	// FIELD AND VARIABLE DECLARATIONS
   129	// =============================================================================
   130	
   131	/**
   132	 * @defgroup java_variables Field and Variable Declarations
   133	 * @brief Fields, local variables, and parameters
   134	 *
   135	 * Java variables are mutable by default. The `final` modifier makes them
   136	 * immutable, but this is handled via modifier nodes rather than refinements.
   137	 *
   138	 * Variable types:
   139	 * - `field_declaration`: Class-level fields (FIELD refinement)
   140	 * - `local_variable_declaration`: Method-local variables (MUTABLE refinement)
   141	 * - `formal_parameter`: Method parameters (PARAMETER refinement)
   142	 * - `enum_constant`: Enum values (FIELD refinement)
   143	 * @{
   144	 */
   145	DEF_TYPE("field_declaration", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::FIELD, FIND_IN_DECLARATOR, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, 0)
   146	DEF_TYPE("local_variable_declaration", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::MUTABLE, FIND_IN_DECLARATOR, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, 0)
   147	DEF_TYPE("variable_declarator", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::MUTABLE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, 0)
   148	DEF_TYPE("formal_parameter", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::PARAMETER, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   149	DEF_TYPE("spread_parameter", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::PARAMETER, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   150	DEF_TYPE("catch_formal_parameter", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::PARAMETER, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   151	DEF_TYPE("enum_constant", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::FIELD, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   152	/** @} */
   153	
   154	// =============================================================================
   155	// METHOD CALLS AND ACCESS
   156	// =============================================================================
   157	
   158	/**
   159	 * @defgroup java_calls Method Calls and Access
   160	 * @brief Method invocations, object creation, and field access
   161	 *
   162	 * Java call expressions:
   163	 * - `method_invocation`: Calling methods on objects (METHOD refinement)
   164	 * - `object_creation_expression`: `new` keyword (CONSTRUCTOR refinement)
   165	 * - `method_reference`: `Class::method` syntax (Java 8+)
   166	 *
   167	 * Field and array access are separate node types.
   168	 * @{
   169	 */
   170	DEF_TYPE("method_invocation", COMPUTATION_CALL | SemanticRefinements::Call::METHOD, FIND_CALL_TARGET, FUNCTION_CALL, 0)
   171	DEF_TYPE("object_creation_expression", COMPUTATION_CALL | SemanticRefinements::Call::CONSTRUCTOR, FIND_CALL_TARGET, FUNCTION_CALL, 0)
   172	DEF_TYPE("method_reference", COMPUTATION_ACCESS, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   173	DEF_TYPE("field_access", COMPUTATION_ACCESS, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   174	DEF_TYPE("array_access", COMPUTATION_ACCESS, NONE, NONE, 0)
   175	/** @} */
   176	
   177	// =============================================================================
   178	// TYPE SYSTEM
   179	// =============================================================================
   180	
   181	/**
   182	 * @defgroup java_type_system Type System
   183	 * @brief Type references, primitives, generics, and arrays
   184	 *
   185	 * Java has both primitive types and reference types:
   186	 * - Primitives: `int`, `boolean`, `float`, etc. (TYPE_PRIMITIVE)
   187	 * - Reference types: Classes, interfaces, arrays (TYPE_REFERENCE)
   188	 * - Generics: Type parameters `<T>` (TYPE_GENERIC)
   189	 * - Arrays: `Type[]` (TYPE_COMPOSITE)
   190	 *
   191	 * The `void` keyword is also treated as a type for return type declarations.
   192	 * @{
   193	 */
   194	
   195	// Type references
   196	DEF_TYPE("type_identifier", TYPE_REFERENCE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   197	DEF_TYPE("scoped_type_identifier", TYPE_REFERENCE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   198	
   199	// Primitive types
   200	DEF_TYPE("integral_type", TYPE_PRIMITIVE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   201	DEF_TYPE("floating_point_type", TYPE_PRIMITIVE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   202	DEF_TYPE("boolean_type", TYPE_PRIMITIVE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   203	DEF_TYPE("void_type", TYPE_PRIMITIVE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   204	
   205	// Composite and generic types
   206	DEF_TYPE("array_type", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   207	DEF_TYPE("dimensions", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   208	DEF_TYPE("generic_type", TYPE_GENERIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   209	DEF_TYPE("type_arguments", TYPE_GENERIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   210	/** @} */
   211	
   212	// =============================================================================
   213	// OPERATORS AND EXPRESSIONS
   214	// =============================================================================
   215	
   216	/**
   217	 * @defgroup java_operators Operators and Expressions
   218	 * @brief Arithmetic, logical, comparison, and assignment operators
   219	 *
   220	 * Java operators include:
   221	 * - Arithmetic: `+`, `-`, `*`, `/`, `%`, bitwise, shifts
   222	 * - Logical: `&&`, `||`, `!`
   223	 * - Comparison: `==`, `!=`, `<`, `>`, `<=`, `>=`, `instanceof`
   224	 * - Assignment: `=`, `+=`, `-=`, etc.
   225	 * - Ternary: `? :`
   226	 * - Update: `++`, `--` (prefix and postfix)
   227	 *
   228	 * Java has unsigned right shift `>>>` unlike C/C++.
   229	 * @{
   230	 */
   231	DEF_TYPE("assignment_expression", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT | SemanticRefinements::Assignment::SIMPLE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   232	DEF_TYPE("binary_expression", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC | SemanticRefinements::Arithmetic::BINARY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   233	DEF_TYPE("unary_expression", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC | SemanticRefinements::Arithmetic::UNARY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   234	DEF_TYPE("update_expression", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC | SemanticRefinements::Arithmetic::UNARY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   235	DEF_TYPE("ternary_expression", FLOW_CONDITIONAL | SemanticRefinements::Conditional::TERNARY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   236	DEF_TYPE("parenthesized_expression", COMPUTATION_EXPRESSION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   237	/** @} */
   238	
   239	// =============================================================================
   240	// IDENTIFIERS AND LITERALS
   241	// =============================================================================
   242	
   243	/**
   244	 * @defgroup java_identifiers Identifiers
   245	 * @brief Identifier and scoped identifier nodes
   246	 * @{
   247	 */
   248	DEF_TYPE("identifier", NAME_IDENTIFIER, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   249	DEF_TYPE("scoped_identifier", NAME_QUALIFIED, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   250	/** @} */
   251	
   252	/**
   253	 * @defgroup java_literals Literal Values
   254	 * @brief Numeric, string, and boolean literals
   255	 *
   256	 * Java numeric literals can have various bases:
   257	 * - Decimal: `123`
   258	 * - Hexadecimal: `0xFF`

     2	 * @file cpp_types.def
     3	 * @brief C++ language node type mappings for AST semantic extraction
     4	 *
     5	 * This file defines the mapping from tree-sitter C++ node types to semantic

     7	 * with object-oriented, generic, and modern language features.
     8	 *
     9	 * @details
    10	 * Each entry uses the DEF_TYPE macro with the following signature:
    11	 * ```cpp
    12	 * DEF_TYPE(raw_type, semantic_type, name_extraction, native_extraction, flags)
    13	 * ```
    14	 *
    15	 * | Parameter | Description |
    16	 * |-----------|-------------|
    17	 * | raw_type | The tree-sitter node type string (e.g., `"class_specifier"`) |
    18	 * | semantic_type | Base semantic category, optionally OR'd with refinement bits |
    19	 * | name_extraction | Strategy enum from `ExtractionStrategy` for extracting node names |
    20	 * | native_extraction | Strategy enum from `NativeExtractionStrategy` for rich context |
    21	 * | flags | Bitwise OR of `ASTNodeFlags` values |
    22	 *
    23	 * ## C++ Language Characteristics
    24	 *
    25	 * - **Multi-paradigm**: OOP, generic, functional, procedural
    26	 * - **Classes**: Full OOP with inheritance, polymorphism, encapsulation
    27	 * - **Templates**: Compile-time generics and metaprogramming
    28	 * - **RAII**: Resource management via constructors/destructors
    29	 * - **References**: Lvalue (`&`) and rvalue (`&&`) references
    30	 * - **Lambdas**: Anonymous functions with captures (C++11)
    31	 * - **Coroutines**: co_await, co_yield, co_return (C++20)
    32	 * - **Namespaces**: Hierarchical scope organization
    33	 * - **Access specifiers**: public, private, protected
    34	 * - **Virtual functions**: Runtime polymorphism with override/final
    35	 * - **Operator overloading**: Custom operator implementations
    36	 * - **Exception handling**: try/catch/throw with RAII cleanup
    37	 *
    38	 * ## Semantic Type Encoding
    39	 *
    40	 * Semantic types use 8-bit encoding:
    41	 * - Bits 7-2: Base category (e.g., `DEFINITION_CLASS = 0x0C`)
    42	 * - Bits 1-0: Refinement within category
    43	 *
    44	 * Example: `DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::CONSTRUCTOR`
    45	 *   - Base: 0x04 (function definition)
    46	 *   - Refinement: 0x02 (constructor)
    47	 *   - Combined: 0x06
    48	 *
    49	 * @see semantic_types.hpp for complete semantic type definitions
    50	 * @see node_config.hpp for extraction strategy enums and refinements
    51	 * @see c_types.def for C base definitions
    52	 */

1	/**
     2	 * @file cpp_types.def
     3	 * @brief C++ language node type mappings for AST semantic extraction
     4	 *
     5	 * This file defines the mapping from tree-sitter C++ node types to semantic
     6	 * categories, extraction strategies, and behavioral flags. C++ extends C
     7	 * with object-oriented, generic, and modern language features.
     8	 *
     9	 * @details
    10	 * Each entry uses the DEF_TYPE macro with the following signature:
    11	 * ```cpp
    12	 * DEF_TYPE(raw_type, semantic_type, name_extraction, native_extraction, flags)
    13	 * ```
    14	 *
    15	 * | Parameter | Description |
    16	 * |-----------|-------------|
    17	 * | raw_type | The tree-sitter node type string (e.g., `"class_specifier"`) |
    18	 * | semantic_type | Base semantic category, optionally OR'd with refinement bits |
    19	 * | name_extraction | Strategy enum from `ExtractionStrategy` for extracting node names |
    20	 * | native_extraction | Strategy enum from `NativeExtractionStrategy` for rich context |
    21	 * | flags | Bitwise OR of `ASTNodeFlags` values |
    22	 *
    23	 * ## C++ Language Characteristics
    24	 *
    25	 * - **Multi-paradigm**: OOP, generic, functional, procedural
    26	 * - **Classes**: Full OOP with inheritance, polymorphism, encapsulation
    27	 * - **Templates**: Compile-time generics and metaprogramming
    28	 * - **RAII**: Resource management via constructors/destructors
    29	 * - **References**: Lvalue (`&`) and rvalue (`&&`) references
    30	 * - **Lambdas**: Anonymous functions with captures (C++11)
    31	 * - **Coroutines**: co_await, co_yield, co_return (C++20)
    32	 * - **Namespaces**: Hierarchical scope organization
    33	 * - **Access specifiers**: public, private, protected
    34	 * - **Virtual functions**: Runtime polymorphism with override/final
    35	 * - **Operator overloading**: Custom operator implementations
    36	 * - **Exception handling**: try/catch/throw with RAII cleanup
    37	 *
    38	 * ## Semantic Type Encoding
    39	 *
    40	 * Semantic types use 8-bit encoding:
    41	 * - Bits 7-2: Base category (e.g., `DEFINITION_CLASS = 0x0C`)
    42	 * - Bits 1-0: Refinement within category
    43	 *
    44	 * Example: `DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::CONSTRUCTOR`
    45	 *   - Base: 0x04 (function definition)
    46	 *   - Refinement: 0x02 (constructor)
    47	 *   - Combined: 0x06
    48	 *
    49	 * @see semantic_types.hpp for complete semantic type definitions
    50	 * @see node_config.hpp for extraction strategy enums and refinements
    51	 * @see c_types.def for C base definitions
    52	 */
    53	
    54	// ============================================================================
    55	// FUNCTION DEFINITIONS
    56	// ============================================================================
    57	
    58	/**
    59	 * @defgroup cpp_functions Function Definitions
    60	 * @brief C++ function and method definitions
    61	 *
    62	 * C++ has regular functions, member functions (methods), constructors,
    63	 * destructors, and lambda expressions. Definitions have bodies (IS_EMBODIED),
    64	 * declarations do not (IS_DECLARATION_ONLY).
    65	 * @{
    66	 */
    67	
    68	/**
    69	 * @brief Function definition: `ReturnType name(params) { body }`
    70	 * Uses CUSTOM for handling qualified names, operator overloads, and destructor names
    71	 */
    72	DEF_TYPE("function_definition", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR, CUSTOM, FUNCTION_WITH_PARAMS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
    73	
    74	/**
    75	 * @brief Function declarator (forward declaration): `ReturnType name(params);`
    76	 * No body - declaration only
    77	 */
    78	DEF_TYPE("function_declarator", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, FUNCTION_WITH_PARAMS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_DECLARATION_ONLY)
    79	
    80	/**
    81	 * @brief Method definition in class body
    82	 */
    83	DEF_TYPE("method_definition", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, FUNCTION_WITH_PARAMS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
    84	
    85	/**
    86	 * @brief Constructor: `ClassName(params) : initializers { body }`
    87	 * Uses CONSTRUCTOR refinement
    88	 */
    89	DEF_TYPE("constructor_definition", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::CONSTRUCTOR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
    90	
    91	/**
    92	 * @brief Destructor: `~ClassName() { body }`
    93	 * Uses CONSTRUCTOR refinement (cleanup counterpart)
    94	 */
    95	DEF_TYPE("destructor_definition", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::CONSTRUCTOR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
    96	
    97	/**
    98	 * @brief Lambda expression: `[captures](params) { body }`
    99	 * Uses LAMBDA refinement
   100	 */
   101	DEF_TYPE("lambda_expression", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::LAMBDA, FIND_ASSIGNMENT_TARGET, ARROW_FUNCTION, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
   102	
   103	/**
   104	 * @brief Template function definition
   105	 */
   106	DEF_TYPE("template_function", DEFINITION_FUNCTION, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   107	
   108	/**
   109	 * @brief Template method definition
   110	 */
   111	DEF_TYPE("template_method", DEFINITION_FUNCTION, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   112	
   113	/**
   114	 * @brief Preprocessor function-like macro
   115	 */
   116	DEF_TYPE("preproc_function_def", DEFINITION_FUNCTION, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   117	
   118	/**
   119	 * @brief Default method clause: `= default`
   120	 */
   121	DEF_TYPE("default_method_clause", DEFINITION_FUNCTION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   122	
   123	/**
   124	 * @brief Delete method clause: `= delete`
   125	 */
   126	DEF_TYPE("delete_method_clause", DEFINITION_FUNCTION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   127	
   128	/** @} */ // end cpp_functions
   129	
   130	// ============================================================================
   131	// CLASS DEFINITIONS
   132	// ============================================================================
   133	
   134	/**
   135	 * @defgroup cpp_classes Class Definitions
   136	 * @brief C++ class, struct, union, and enum types
   137	 *
   138	 * C++ classes support inheritance, access control, and polymorphism.
   139	 * All class-like constructs have bodies (IS_EMBODIED).
   140	 * @{
   141	 */
   142	
   143	/**
   144	 * @brief Class definition: `class Name : public Base { members };`
   145	 */
   146	DEF_TYPE("class_specifier", DEFINITION_CLASS, FIND_IDENTIFIER, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
   147	
   148	/**
   149	 * @brief Struct definition: `struct Name { members };`
   150	 * Default public access (vs class default private)
   151	 */
   152	DEF_TYPE("struct_specifier", DEFINITION_CLASS, FIND_IDENTIFIER, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
   153	
   154	/**
   155	 * @brief Union definition: `union Name { members };`
   156	 */
   157	DEF_TYPE("union_specifier", DEFINITION_CLASS, FIND_IDENTIFIER, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
   158	
   159	/**
   160	 * @brief Enum definition: `enum Name { values };` or `enum class Name { values };`
   161	 */
   162	DEF_TYPE("enum_specifier", DEFINITION_CLASS, FIND_IDENTIFIER, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
   163	
   164	/**
   165	 * @brief Type definition (typedef or using)
   166	 */
   167	DEF_TYPE("type_definition", DEFINITION_CLASS, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   168	
   169	/**
   170	 * @brief Base class clause: `: public Base, private Other`
   171	 */
   172	DEF_TYPE("base_class_clause", TYPE_REFERENCE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   173	
   174	/** @} */ // end cpp_classes
   175	
   176	// ============================================================================
   177	// VARIABLE DECLARATIONS
   178	// ============================================================================
   179	
   180	/**
   181	 * @defgroup cpp_variables Variable Declarations
   182	 * @brief Variable, parameter, and field declarations
   183	 * @{
   184	 */
   185	
   186	/**
   187	 * @brief Variable declaration: `Type name = value;`
   188	 */
   189	DEF_TYPE("declaration", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::MUTABLE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   190	
   191	/**
   192	 * @brief Function parameter: `Type name` in parameter list
   193	 */
   194	DEF_TYPE("parameter_declaration", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::PARAMETER, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   195	
   196	/**
   197	 * @brief Optional parameter with default: `Type name = default`
   198	 */
   199	DEF_TYPE("optional_parameter_declaration", DEFINITION_VARIABLE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   200	
   201	/**
   202	 * @brief Field declaration in class: `Type name;`
   203	 */
   204	DEF_TYPE("field_declaration", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::FIELD, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   205	
   206	/**
   207	 * @brief Initializing declarator: `name = value` in declaration
   208	 */
   209	DEF_TYPE("init_declarator", DEFINITION_VARIABLE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   210	
   211	/**
   212	 * @brief Enumerator: `NAME` or `NAME = value` in enum
   213	 */
   214	DEF_TYPE("enumerator", DEFINITION_VARIABLE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   215	
   216	/**
   217	 * @brief Type alias: `using Name = Type;`
   218	 */
   219	DEF_TYPE("alias_declaration", DEFINITION_VARIABLE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   220	
   221	/** @} */ // end cpp_variables
   222	
   223	// ============================================================================
   224	// NAMESPACE AND MODULES
   225	// ============================================================================
   226	
   227	/**
   228	 * @defgroup cpp_namespaces Namespaces and Modules
   229	 * @brief Code organization constructs
   230	 * @{
   231	 */
   232	
   233	/**
   234	 * @brief Namespace definition: `namespace Name { ... }`
   235	 */
   236	DEF_TYPE("namespace_definition", DEFINITION_MODULE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
   237

   242	
   243	/** @} */ // end cpp_namespaces
   244	
   245	// ============================================================================

   249	/**

   252	 * @{
   253	 */

   258	DEF_TYPE("call_expression", COMPUTATION_CALL | SemanticRefinements::Call::FUNCTION, FIND_CALL_TARGET, FUNCTION_CALL, 0)
   259	
   260	/**
   261	 * @brief Constructor call: `new Type(args)`
   262	 */
   263	DEF_TYPE("new_expression", COMPUTATION_CALL | SemanticRefinements::Call::CONSTRUCTOR, FIND_CALL_TARGET, FUNCTION_CALL, 0)
   264	
   265	/**

   268	DEF_TYPE("delete_expression", COMPUTATION_CALL | SemanticRefinements::Call::FUNCTION, FIND_CALL_TARGET, FUNCTION_CALL, 0)
   269	
   270	/**

   272	 */
   273	DEF_TYPE("binary_expression", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC | SemanticRefinements::Arithmetic::BINARY, NONE, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, 0)
   274

   278	DEF_TYPE("unary_expression", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC | SemanticRefinements::Arithmetic::UNARY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   279

   289

   299

   303	DEF_TYPE("expression_statement", EXECUTION_STATEMENT, NONE, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, 0)

{"result":"     1	/**
     2	 * @file c_types.def
     3	 * @brief C language node type mappings for AST semantic extraction
     4	 *
     5	 * This file defines the mapping from tree-sitter C node types to semantic
     6	 * categories, extraction strategies, and behavioral flags. C is the base
     7	 * for C++, which extends these definitions.
     8	 *
     9	 * @details
    10	 * Each entry uses the DEF_TYPE macro with the following signature:
    11	 * ```cpp
    12	 * DEF_TYPE(raw_type, semantic_type, name_extraction, native_extraction, flags)
    13	 * ```
    14	 *
    15	 * | Parameter | Description |
    16	 * |-----------|-------------|
    17	 * | raw_type | The tree-sitter node type string (e.g., `"function_definition"`) |
    18	 * | semantic_type | Base semantic category, optionally OR'd with refinement bits |
    19	 * | name_extraction | Strategy enum from `ExtractionStrategy` for extracting node names |
    20	 * | native_extraction | Strategy enum from `NativeExtractionStrategy` for rich context |
    21	 * | flags | Bitwise OR of `ASTNodeFlags` values |
    22	 *
    23	 * ## C Language Characteristics
    24	 *
    25	 * - **Low-level systems language**: Direct memory access, pointer arithmetic
    26	 * - **Static typing**: Compile-time type checking
    27	 * - **Manual memory management**: malloc/free, no garbage collection
    28	 * - **Preprocessor**: Text substitution before compilation (#include, #define)
    29	 * - **Structs/unions/enums**: Aggregate data types (no classes)
    30	 * - **Pointers**: First-class support for memory addresses
    31	 * - **Function definitions vs declarations**: Prototypes vs implementations
    32	 * - **Storage classes**: static, extern, register, auto
    33	 * - **Type qualifiers**: const, volatile, restrict
    34	 * - **No function overloading**: Each function has unique name
    35	 *
    36	 * ## Semantic Type Encoding
    37	 *
    38	 * Semantic types use 8-bit encoding:
    39	 * - Bits 7-2: Base category (e.g., `DEFINITION_FUNCTION = 0x04`)
    40	 * - Bits 1-0: Refinement within category
    41	 *
    42	 * Example: `DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR`
    43	 *   - Base: 0x04 (function definition)
    44	 *   - Refinement: 0x00 (regular function)
    45	 *   - Combined: 0x04
    46	 *
    47	 * @see semantic_types.hpp for complete semantic type definitions
    48	 * @see node_config.hpp for extraction strategy enums and refinements
    49	 * @see cpp_types.def for C++ extensions
    50	 */
    51	
    52	// ============================================================================
    53	// TRANSLATION UNIT
    54	// ============================================================================
    55	
    56	/**
    57	 * @defgroup c_translation_unit Translation Unit
    58	 * @brief Top-level compilation unit
    59	 * @{
    60	 */
    61	
    62	/**
    63	 * @brief Translation unit: the root of a C source file
    64	 * Represents an entire .c or .h file
    65	 */
    66	DEF_TYPE("translation_unit", DEFINITION_MODULE, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
    67	
    68	/** @} */ // end c_translation_unit
    69	
    70	// ============================================================================
    71	// PREPROCESSOR DIRECTIVES
    72	// ============================================================================
    73	
    74	/**
    75	 * @defgroup c_preprocessor Preprocessor Directives
    76	 * @brief C preprocessor constructs (#include, #define, etc.)
    77	 *
    78	 * The C preprocessor performs text substitution before compilation.
    79	 * These nodes represent preprocessor directives and macro expansions.
    80	 * @{
    81	 */
    82	
    83	/**
    84	 * @brief #include directive: `#include <header.h>` or `#include "header.h"`
    85	 */
    86	DEF_TYPE("preproc_include", EXTERNAL_IMPORT | SemanticRefinements::Import::MODULE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
    87	
    88	/**
    89	 * @brief Object-like macro: `#define NAME value`
    90	 * Treated as immutable constant
    91	 */
    92	DEF_TYPE("preproc_def", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::IMMUTABLE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
    93	
    94	/**
    95	 * @brief Function-like macro: `#define NAME(args) body`
    96	 */
    97	DEF_TYPE("preproc_function_def", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
    98	
    99	/**
   100	 * @brief Macro invocation: `MACRO(args)`
   101	 * Uses MACRO refinement for Call semantic type
   102	 */
   103	DEF_TYPE("preproc_call", COMPUTATION_CALL | SemanticRefinements::Call::MACRO, FIND_CALL_TARGET, NONE, 0)
   104	
   105	/**
   106	 * @brief Conditional compilation: `#if condition`
   107	 */
   108	DEF_TYPE("preproc_if", FLOW_CONDITIONAL | SemanticRefinements::Conditional::BINARY, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   109	
   110	/**
   111	 * @brief Conditional compilation: `#ifdef MACRO`
   112	 */
   113	DEF_TYPE("preproc_ifdef", FLOW_CONDITIONAL | SemanticRefinements::Conditional::BINARY, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   114	
   115	/**
   116	 * @brief Conditional else: `#else`
   117	 */
   118	DEF_TYPE("preproc_else", FLOW_CONDITIONAL | SemanticRefinements::Conditional::BINARY, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   119	
   120	/**
   121	 * @brief Conditional elif: `#elif condition`
   122	 */
   123	DEF_TYPE("preproc_elif", FLOW_CONDITIONAL | SemanticRefinements::Conditional::BINARY, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   124	
   125	/**
   126	 * @brief Preprocessor parameters in function-like macro
   127	 */
   128	DEF_TYPE("preproc_params", ORGANIZATION_LIST | SemanticRefinements::Organization::COLLECTION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   129	
   130	/**
   131	 * @brief Preprocessor argument text
   132	 */
   133	DEF_TYPE("preproc_arg", METADATA_DIRECTIVE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   134	
   135	/**
   136	 * @brief defined() operator in preprocessor conditionals
   137	 */
   138	DEF_TYPE("defined", METADATA_DIRECTIVE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   139	
   140	/**
   141	 * @brief Preprocessor defined check
   142	 */
   143	DEF_TYPE("preproc_defined", METADATA_DIRECTIVE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   144	
   145	/**
   146	 * @brief Generic preprocessor directive
   147	 */
   148	DEF_TYPE("preproc_directive", METADATA_DIRECTIVE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   149	
   150	/** @} */ // end c_preprocessor
   151	
   152	// ============================================================================
   153	// DECLARATIONS
   154	// ============================================================================
   155	
   156	/**
   157	 * @defgroup c_declarations Declarations
   158	 * @brief Function definitions, variable declarations, type definitions
   159	 *
   160	 * C distinguishes between declarations (prototypes) and definitions
   161	 * (implementations). Function definitions have bodies (IS_EMBODIED).
   162	 * @{
   163	 */
   164	
   165	/**
   166	 * @brief Function definition: `type name(params) { body }`
   167	 * Has a body (IS_EMBODIED); uses FIND_IN_DECLARATOR for complex declarators
   168	 */
   169	DEF_TYPE("function_definition", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR, FIND_IN_DECLARATOR, FUNCTION_WITH_PARAMS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
   170	
   171	/**
   172	 * @brief Variable/function declaration: `type name;` or `type name(params);`
   173	 */
   174	DEF_TYPE("declaration", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::MUTABLE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   175	
   176	/**
   177	 * @brief Struct definition: `struct name { fields };`
   178	 */
   179	DEF_TYPE("struct_specifier", DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   180	
   181	/**
   182	 * @brief Union definition: `union name { fields };`
   183	 * Tagged union type where members share memory
   184	 */
   185	DEF_TYPE("union_specifier", DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   186	
   187	/**
   188	 * @brief Enum definition: `enum name { values };`
   189	 */
   190	DEF_TYPE("enum_specifier", DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::ENUM, FIND_IDENTIFIER, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   191	
   192	/**
   193	 * @brief Typedef: `typedef old_type new_name;`
   194	 */
   195	DEF_TYPE("typedef_declaration", DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   196	
   197	/**
   198	 * @brief Type definition (alternate form)
   199	 */
   200	DEF_TYPE("type_definition", DEFINITION_CLASS, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   201	
   202	/**
   203	 * @brief Field in struct/union: `type name;`
   204	 */
   205	DEF_TYPE("field_declaration", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::FIELD, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   206	
   207	/**
   208	 * @brief Enumerator: `NAME` or `NAME = value` in enum
   209	 */
   210	DEF_TYPE("enumerator", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::FIELD, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   211	
   212	/**
   213	 * @brief Function parameter: `type name` in parameter list
   214	 */
   215	DEF_TYPE("parameter_declaration", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::PARAMETER, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   216	
   217	/**
   218	 * @brief Initializing declarator: `name = value` in declaration
   219	 */
   220	DEF_TYPE("init_declarator", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::MUTABLE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   221	
   222	/** @} */ // end c_declarations
   223	
   224	// ============================================================================
   225	// TYPE SPECIFIERS
   226	// ============================================================================
   227	
   228	/**
   229	 * @defgroup c_types Type Specifiers
   230	 * @brief Type system nodes
   231	 *
   232	 * C has primitive types, user-defined types, and compound types
   233	 * built with pointers, arrays, and function types.
   234	 * @{
   235	 */
   236	
   237	/**
   238	 * @brief Primitive type: int, char, float, double, void, etc.
   239	 */
   240	DEF_TYPE("primitive_type", TYPE_PRIMITIVE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   241	
   242	/**
   243	 * @brief User-defined type reference: struct/union/enum/typedef name
   244	 */
   245	DEF_TYPE("type_identifier", TYPE_REFERENCE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   246	
   247	/**
   248	 * @brief Sized type specifier: `long int`, `short int`, etc.
   249	 */
   250	DEF_TYPE("sized_type_specifier", TYPE_PRIMITIVE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   251	
   252	/**
   253	 * @brief Pointer declarator: `*name`
   254	 */
   255	DEF_TYPE("pointer_declarator", TYPE_COMPOSITE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   256	
   257	/**
   258	 * @brief Array declarator: `name[size]`
   259	 */
   260	DEF_TYPE("array_declarator", TYPE_COMPOSITE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   261	
   262	/**
   263	 * @brief Function declarator: `name(params)`
   264	 */
   265	DEF_TYPE("function_declarator", TYPE_COMPOSITE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   266	
   267	/**
   268	 * @brief Abstract declarator (type without name)
   269	 */
   270	DEF_TYPE("abstract_declarator", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   271	
   272	/**
   273	 * @brief Parenthesized declarator: `(*name)`
   274	 */
   275	DEF_TYPE("parenthesized_declarator", TYPE_COMPOSITE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   276	
   277	/**
   278	 * @brief Abstract pointer declarator
   279	 */
   280	DEF_TYPE("abstract_pointer_declarator", TYPE_REFERENCE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   281	
   282	/**
   283	 * @brief Abstract parenthesized declarator
   284	 */
   285	DEF_TYPE("abstract_parenthesized_declarator", TYPE_REFERENCE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   286	
   287	/**
   288	 * @brief Abstract array declarator
   289	 */
   290	DEF_TYPE("abstract_array_declarator", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   291	
   292	/**
   293	 * @brief Abstract function declarator
   294	 */
   295	DEF_TYPE("abstract_function_declarator", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   296	
   297	/**
   298	 * @brief Type descriptor in expressions
   299	 */
   300	DEF_TYPE("type_descriptor", TYPE_REFERENCE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   301	
   302	/**
   303	 * @brief Macro used as type specifier
   304	 */
   305	DEF_TYPE("macro_type_specifier", TYPE_REFERENCE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   306	
   307	/**
   308	 * @brief Bitfield specification: `: width`
   309	 */
   310	DEF_TYPE("bitfield_clause", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   311	
   312	/** @} */ // end c_types
   313	
   314	// ============================================================================
   315	// STATEMENTS
   316	// ============================================================================
   317	
   318	/**
   319	 * @defgroup c_statements Statements
   320	 * @brief Control flow and executable statements
   321	 * @{
   322	 */
   323	
   324	/**
   325	 * @brief Compound statement (block): `{ statements }`
   326	 */
   327	DEF_TYPE("compound_statement", ORGANIZATION_BLOCK | SemanticRefinements::Organization::SEQUENTIAL, NONE, NONE, 0)
   328	
   329	/**

   331	 */

   333	
   334	/**

   338

{"result":"     1	/**
     2	 * @file c_types.def
     3	 * @brief C language node type mappings for AST semantic extraction
     4	 *
     5	 * This file defines the mapping from tree-sitter C node types to semantic
     6	 * categories, extraction strategies, and behavioral flags. C is the base
     7	 * for C++, which extends these definitions.
     8	 *
     9	 * @details
    10	 * Each entry uses the DEF_TYPE macro with the following signature:
    11	 * ```cpp
    12	 * DEF_TYPE(raw_type, semantic_type, name_extraction, native_extraction, flags)
    13	 * ```
    14	 *
    15	 * | Parameter | Description |
    16	 * |-----------|-------------|
    17	 * | raw_type | The tree-sitter node type string (e.g., `"function_definition"`) |
    18	 * | semantic_type | Base semantic category, optionally OR'd with refinement bits |
    19	 * | name_extraction | Strategy enum from `ExtractionStrategy` for extracting node names |
    20	 * | native_extraction | Strategy enum from `NativeExtractionStrategy` for rich context |
    21	 * | flags | Bitwise OR of `ASTNodeFlags` values |
    22	 *
    23	 * ## C Language Characteristics
    24	 *
    25	 * - **Low-level systems language**: Direct memory access, pointer arithmetic
    26	 * - **Static typing**: Compile-time type checking
    27	 * - **Manual memory management**: malloc/free, no garbage collection
    28	 * - **Preprocessor**: Text substitution before compilation (#include, #define)
    29	 * - **Structs/unions/enums**: Aggregate data types (no classes)
    30	 * - **Pointers**: First-class support for memory addresses
    31	 * - **Function definitions vs declarations**: Prototypes vs implementations
    32	 * - **Storage classes**: static, extern, register, auto
    33	 * - **Type qualifiers**: const, volatile, restrict
    34	 * - **No function overloading**: Each function has unique name
    35	 *
    36	 * ## Semantic Type Encoding
    37	 *
    38	 * Semantic types use 8-bit encoding:
    39	 * - Bits 7-2: Base category (e.g., `DEFINITION_FUNCTION = 0x04`)
    40	 * - Bits 1-0: Refinement within category
    41	 *
    42	 * Example: `DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR`
    43	 *   - Base: 0x04 (function definition)
    44	 *   - Refinement: 0x00 (regular function)
    45	 *   - Combined: 0x04
    46	 *
    47	 * @see semantic_types.hpp for complete semantic type definitions
    48	 * @see node_config.hpp for extraction strategy enums and refinements
    49	 * @see cpp_types.def for C++ extensions
    50	 */
    51	
    52	// ============================================================================
    53	// TRANSLATION UNIT
    54	// ============================================================================
    55	
    56	/**
    57	 * @defgroup c_translation_unit Translation Unit
    58	 * @brief Top-level compilation unit
    59	 * @{
    60	 */
    61	
    62	/**
    63	 * @brief Translation unit: the root of a C source file
    64	 * Represents an entire .c or .h file
    65	 */
    66	DEF_TYPE("translation_unit", DEFINITION_MODULE, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
    67	
    68	/** @} */ // end c_translation_unit
    69	
    70	// ============================================================================
    71	// PREPROCESSOR DIRECTIVES
    72	// ============================================================================
    73	
    74	/**
    75	 * @defgroup c_preprocessor Preprocessor Directives
    76	 * @brief C preprocessor constructs (#include, #define, etc.)
    77	 *
    78	 * The C preprocessor performs text substitution before compilation.
    79	 * These nodes represent preprocessor directives and macro expansions.
    80	 * @{
    81	 */
    82	
    83	/**
    84	 * @brief #include directive: `#include <header.h>` or `#include "header.h"`
    85	 */
    86	DEF_TYPE("preproc_include", EXTERNAL_IMPORT | SemanticRefinements::Import::MODULE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
    87	
    88	/**
    89	 * @brief Object-like macro: `#define NAME value`
    90	 * Treated as immutable constant
    91	 */
    92	DEF_TYPE("preproc_def", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::IMMUTABLE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
    93	
    94	/**
    95	 * @brief Function-like macro: `#define NAME(args) body`
    96	 */
    97	DEF_TYPE("preproc_function_def", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
    98	
    99	/**
   100	 * @brief Macro invocation: `MACRO(args)`
   101	 * Uses MACRO refinement for Call semantic type
   102	 */
   103	DEF_TYPE("preproc_call", COMPUTATION_CALL | SemanticRefinements::Call::MACRO, FIND_CALL_TARGET, NONE, 0)
   104	
   105	/**
   106	 * @brief Conditional compilation: `#if condition`
   107	 */
   108	DEF_TYPE("preproc_if", FLOW_CONDITIONAL | SemanticRefinements::Conditional::BINARY, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   109	
   110	/**
   111	 * @brief Conditional compilation: `#ifdef MACRO`
   112	 */
   113	DEF_TYPE("preproc_ifdef", FLOW_CONDITIONAL | SemanticRefinements::Conditional::BINARY, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   114	
   115	/**
   116	 * @brief Conditional else: `#else`
   117	 */
   118	DEF_TYPE("preproc_else", FLOW_CONDITIONAL | SemanticRefinements::Conditional::BINARY, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   119	
   120	/**
   121	 * @brief Conditional elif: `#elif condition`
   122	 */
   123	DEF_TYPE("preproc_elif", FLOW_CONDITIONAL | SemanticRefinements::Conditional::BINARY, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   124	
   125	/**
   126	 * @brief Preprocessor parameters in function-like macro
   127	 */
   128	DEF_TYPE("preproc_params", ORGANIZATION_LIST | SemanticRefinements::Organization::COLLECTION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   129	
   130	/**
   131	 * @brief Preprocessor argument text
   132	 */
   133	DEF_TYPE("preproc_arg", METADATA_DIRECTIVE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   134	
   135	/**
   136	 * @brief defined() operator in preprocessor conditionals
   137	 */
   138	DEF_TYPE("defined", METADATA_DIRECTIVE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   139	
   140	/**
   141	 * @brief Preprocessor defined check
   142	 */
   143	DEF_TYPE("preproc_defined", METADATA_DIRECTIVE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   144	
   145	/**
   146	 * @brief Generic preprocessor directive
   147	 */
   148	DEF_TYPE("preproc_directive", METADATA_DIRECTIVE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   149	
   150	/** @} */ // end c_preprocessor
   151	
   152	// ============================================================================
   153	// DECLARATIONS
   154	// ============================================================================
   155	
   156	/**
   157	 * @defgroup c_declarations Declarations
   158	 * @brief Function definitions, variable declarations, type definitions
   159	 *
   160	 * C distinguishes between declarations (prototypes) and definitions
   161	 * (implementations). Function definitions have bodies (IS_EMBODIED).
   162	 * @{
   163	 */
   164	
   165	/**
   166	 * @brief Function definition: `type name(params) { body }`
   167	 * Has a body (IS_EMBODIED); uses FIND_IN_DECLARATOR for complex declarators
   168	 */
   169	DEF_TYPE("function_definition", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR, FIND_IN_DECLARATOR, FUNCTION_WITH_PARAMS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
   170	
   171	/**
   172	 * @brief Variable/function declaration: `type name;` or `type name(params);`
   173	 */
   174	DEF_TYPE("declaration", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::MUTABLE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   175	
   176	/**
   177	 * @brief Struct definition: `struct name { fields };`
   178	 */
   179	DEF_TYPE("struct_specifier", DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   180	
   181	/**
   182	 * @brief Union definition: `union name { fields };`
   183	 * Tagged union type where members share memory
   184	 */
   185	DEF_TYPE("union_specifier", DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   186	
   187	/**
   188	 * @brief Enum definition: `enum name { values };`
   189	 */
   190	DEF_TYPE("enum_specifier", DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::ENUM, FIND_IDENTIFIER, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   191	
   192	/**
   193	 * @brief Typedef: `typedef old_type new_name;`
   194	 */
   195	DEF_TYPE("typedef_declaration", DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   196	
   197	/**
   198	 * @brief Type definition (alternate form)
   199	 */
   200	DEF_TYPE("type_definition", DEFINITION_CLASS, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   201	
   202	/**
   203	 * @brief Field in struct/union: `type name;`
   204	 */
   205	DEF_TYPE("field_declaration", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::FIELD, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   206	
   207	/**
   208	 * @brief Enumerator: `NAME` or `NAME = value` in enum
   209	 */
   210	DEF_TYPE("enumerator", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::FIELD, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   211	
   212	/**
   213	 * @brief Function parameter: `type name` in parameter list
   214	 */
   215	DEF_TYPE("parameter_declaration", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::PARAMETER, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   216	
   217	/**
   218	 * @brief Initializing declarator: `name = value` in declaration
   219	 */
   220	DEF_TYPE("init_declarator", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::MUTABLE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   221	
   222	/** @} */ // end c_declarations
   223	
   224	// ============================================================================
   225	// TYPE SPECIFIERS
   226	// ============================================================================
   227	
   228	/**
   229	 * @defgroup c_types Type Specifiers
   230	 * @brief Type system nodes
   231	 *
   232	 * C has primitive types, user-defined types, and compound types
   233	 * built with pointers, arrays, and function types.
   234	 * @{
   235	 */
   236	
   237	/**
   238	 * @brief Primitive type: int, char, float, double, void, etc.
   239	 */
   240	DEF_TYPE("primitive_type", TYPE_PRIMITIVE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   241	
   242	/**
   243	 * @brief User-defined type reference: struct/union/enum/typedef name
   244	 */
   245	DEF_TYPE("type_identifier", TYPE_REFERENCE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   246	
   247	/**
   248	 * @brief Sized type specifier: `long int`, `short int`, etc.
   249	 */
   250	DEF_TYPE("sized_type_specifier", TYPE_PRIMITIVE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   251	
   252	/**
   253	 * @brief Pointer declarator: `*name`
   254	 */
   255	DEF_TYPE("pointer_declarator", TYPE_COMPOSITE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   256	
   257	/**
   258	 * @brief Array declarator: `name[size]`
   259	 */
   260	DEF_TYPE("array_declarator", TYPE_COMPOSITE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   261	
   262	/**
   263	 * @brief Function declarator: `name(params)`
   264	 */
   265	DEF_TYPE("function_declarator", TYPE_COMPOSITE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   266	
   267	/**
   268	 * @brief Abstract declarator (type without name)
   269	 */
   270	DEF_TYPE("abstract_declarator", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   271	
   272	/**
   273	 * @brief Parenthesized declarator: `(*name)`
   274	 */
   275	DEF_TYPE("parenthesized_declarator", TYPE_COMPOSITE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   276	
   277	/**
   278	 * @brief Abstract pointer declarator
   279	 */
   280	DEF_TYPE("abstract_pointer_declarator", TYPE_REFERENCE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   281	
   282	/**
   283	 * @brief Abstract parenthesized declarator
   284	 */
   285	DEF_TYPE("abstract_parenthesized_declarator", TYPE_REFERENCE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   286	
   287	/**
   288	 * @brief Abstract array declarator
   289	 */
   290	DEF_TYPE("abstract_array_declarator", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   291	
   292	/**
   293	 * @brief Abstract function declarator
   294	 */
   295	DEF_TYPE("abstract_function_declarator", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   296	
   297	/**

1	/**
     2	 * @file c_types.def
     3	 * @brief C language node type mappings for AST semantic extraction
     4	 *
     5	 * This file defines the mapping from tree-sitter C node types to semantic
     6	 * categories, extraction strategies, and behavioral flags. C is the base
     7	 * for C++, which extends these definitions.
     8	 *
     9	 * @details
    10	 * Each entry uses the DEF_TYPE macro with the following signature:
    11	 * ```cpp
    12	 * DEF_TYPE(raw_type, semantic_type, name_extraction, native_extraction, flags)
    13	 * ```
    14	 *
    15	 * | Parameter | Description |
    16	 * |-----------|-------------|
    17	 * | raw_type | The tree-sitter node type string (e.g., `"function_definition"`) |
    18	 * | semantic_type | Base semantic category, optionally OR'd with refinement bits |
    19	 * | name_extraction | Strategy enum from `ExtractionStrategy` for extracting node names |
    20	 * | native_extraction | Strategy enum from `NativeExtractionStrategy` for rich context |
    21	 * | flags | Bitwise OR of `ASTNodeFlags` values |
    22	 *
    23	 * ## C Language Characteristics
    24	 *
    25	 * - **Low-level systems language**: Direct memory access, pointer arithmetic
    26	 * - **Static typing**: Compile-time type checking
    27	 * - **Manual memory management**: malloc/free, no garbage collection
    28	 * - **Preprocessor**: Text substitution before compilation (#include, #define)
    29	 * - **Structs/unions/enums**: Aggregate data types (no classes)
    30	 * - **Pointers**: First-class support for memory addresses
    31	 * - **Function definitions vs declarations**: Prototypes vs implementations
    32	 * - **Storage classes**: static, extern, register, auto
    33	 * - **Type qualifiers**: const, volatile, restrict
    34	 * - **No function overloading**: Each function has unique name
    35	 *
    36	 * ## Semantic Type Encoding
    37	 *
    38	 * Semantic types use 8-bit encoding:
    39	 * - Bits 7-2: Base category (e.g., `DEFINITION_FUNCTION = 0x04`)
    40	 * - Bits 1-0: Refinement within category
    41	 *
    42	 * Example: `DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR`
    43	 *   - Base: 0x04 (function definition)
    44	 *   - Refinement: 0x00 (regular function)
    45	 *   - Combined: 0x04
    46	 *
    47	 * @see semantic_types.hpp for complete semantic type definitions
    48	 * @see node_config.hpp for extraction strategy enums and refinements
    49	 * @see cpp_types.def for C++ extensions
    50	 */
    51	
    52	// ============================================================================
    53	// TRANSLATION UNIT
    54	// ============================================================================
    55	
    56	/**
    57	 * @defgroup c_translation_unit Translation Unit
    58	 * @brief Top-level compilation unit
    59	 * @{
    60	 */
    61	
    62	/**
    63	 * @brief Translation unit: the root of a C source file
    64	 * Represents an entire .c or .h file
    65	 */
    66	DEF_TYPE("translation_unit", DEFINITION_MODULE, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
    67	
    68	/** @} */ // end c_translation_unit
    69	
    70	// ============================================================================
    71	// PREPROCESSOR DIRECTIVES
    72	// ============================================================================
    73	
    74	/**
    75	 * @defgroup c_preprocessor Preprocessor Directives
    76	 * @brief C preprocessor constructs (#include, #define, etc.)
    77	 *
    78	 * The C preprocessor performs text substitution before compilation.
    79	 * These nodes represent preprocessor directives and macro expansions.
    80	 * @{
    81	 */
    82	
    83	/**
    84	 * @brief #include directive: `#include <header.h>` or `#include "header.h"`
    85	 */
    86	DEF_TYPE("preproc_include", EXTERNAL_IMPORT | SemanticRefinements::Import::MODULE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
    87	
    88	/**
    89	 * @brief Object-like macro: `#define NAME value`
    90	 * Treated as immutable constant
    91	 */
    92	DEF_TYPE("preproc_def", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::IMMUTABLE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
    93	
    94	/**
    95	 * @brief Function-like macro: `#define NAME(args) body`
    96	 */
    97	DEF_TYPE("preproc_function_def", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
    98	
    99	/**
   100	 * @brief Macro invocation: `MACRO(args)`
   101	 * Uses MACRO refinement for Call semantic type
   102	 */
   103	DEF_TYPE("preproc_call", COMPUTATION_CALL | SemanticRefinements::Call::MACRO, FIND_CALL_TARGET, NONE, 0)
   104	
   105	/**
   106	 * @brief Conditional compilation: `#if condition`
   107	 */
   108	DEF_TYPE("preproc_if", FLOW_CONDITIONAL | SemanticRefinements::Conditional::BINARY, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   109	
   110	/**
   111	 * @brief Conditional compilation: `#ifdef MACRO`
   112	 */
   113	DEF_TYPE("preproc_ifdef", FLOW_CONDITIONAL | SemanticRefinements::Conditional::BINARY, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   114	
   115	/**
   116	 * @brief Conditional else: `#else`
   117	 */
   118	DEF_TYPE("preproc_else", FLOW_CONDITIONAL | SemanticRefinements::Conditional::BINARY, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   119	
   120	/**
   121	 * @brief Conditional elif: `#elif condition`
   122	 */
   123	DEF_TYPE("preproc_elif", FLOW_CONDITIONAL | SemanticRefinements::Conditional::BINARY, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   124	
   125	/**
   126	 * @brief Preprocessor parameters in function-like macro
   127	 */
   128	DEF_TYPE("preproc_params", ORGANIZATION_LIST | SemanticRefinements::Organization::COLLECTION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   129	
   130	/**
   131	 * @brief Preprocessor argument text
   132	 */
   133	DEF_TYPE("preproc_arg", METADATA_DIRECTIVE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   134	
   135	/**
   136	 * @brief defined() operator in preprocessor conditionals
   137	 */
   138	DEF_TYPE("defined", METADATA_DIRECTIVE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   139	
   140	/**
   141	 * @brief Preprocessor defined check
   142	 */
   143	DEF_TYPE("preproc_defined", METADATA_DIRECTIVE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   144	
   145	/**
   146	 * @brief Generic preprocessor directive
   147	 */
   148	DEF_TYPE("preproc_directive", METADATA_DIRECTIVE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   149	
   150	/** @} */ // end c_preprocessor
   151	
   152	// ============================================================================
   153	// DECLARATIONS
   154	// ============================================================================
   155	
   156	/**
   157	 * @defgroup c_declarations Declarations
   158	 * @brief Function definitions, variable declarations, type definitions
   159	 *
   160	 * C distinguishes between declarations (prototypes) and definitions
   161	 * (implementations). Function definitions have bodies (IS_EMBODIED).
   162	 * @{
   163	 */
   164	
   165	/**
   166	 * @brief Function definition: `type name(params) { body }`
   167	 * Has a body (IS_EMBODIED); uses FIND_IN_DECLARATOR for complex declarators
   168	 */
   169	DEF_TYPE("function_definition", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR, FIND_IN_DECLARATOR, FUNCTION_WITH_PARAMS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
   170	
   171	/**
   172	 * @brief Variable/function declaration: `type name;` or `type name(params);`
   173	 */
   174	DEF_TYPE("declaration", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::MUTABLE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   175	
   176	/**
   177	 * @brief Struct definition: `struct name { fields };`
   178	 */
   179	DEF_TYPE("struct_specifier", DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   180	
   181	/**
   182	 * @brief Union definition: `union name { fields };`
   183	 * Tagged union type where members share memory
   184	 */
   185	DEF_TYPE("union_specifier", DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   186	
   187	/**
   188	 * @brief Enum definition: `enum name { values };`
   189	 */
   190	DEF_TYPE("enum_specifier", DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::ENUM, FIND_IDENTIFIER, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   191	
   192	/**
   193	 * @brief Typedef: `typedef old_type new_name;`
   194	 */
   195	DEF_TYPE("typedef_declaration", DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   196	
   197	/**
   198	 * @brief Type definition (alternate form)
   199	 */
   200	DEF_TYPE("type_definition", DEFINITION_CLASS, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   201	
   202	/**
   203	 * @brief Field in struct/union: `type name;`
   204	 */
   205	DEF_TYPE("field_declaration", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::FIELD, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   206	
   207	/**
   208	 * @brief Enumerator: `NAME` or `NAME = value` in enum
   209	 */
   210	DEF_TYPE("enumerator", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::FIELD, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   211	
   212	/**
   213	 * @brief Function parameter: `type name` in parameter list
   214	 */
   215	DEF_TYPE("parameter_declaration", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::PARAMETER, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   216	
   217	/**
   218	 * @brief Initializing declarator: `name = value` in declaration
   219	 */
   220	DEF_TYPE("init_declarator", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::MUTABLE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   221	
   222	/** @} */ // end c_declarations
   223	
   224	// ============================================================================
   225	// TYPE SPECIFIERS
   226	// ============================================================================
   227	
   228	/**
   229	 * @defgroup c_types Type Specifiers
   230	 * @brief Type system nodes
   231	 *
   232	 * C has primitive types, user-defined types, and compound types
   233	 * built with pointers, arrays, and function types.
   234	 * @{
   235	 */
   236	
   237	/**
   238	 * @brief Primitive type: int, char, float, double, void, etc.
   239	 */
   240	DEF_TYPE("primitive_type", TYPE_PRIMITIVE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   241	
   242	/**
   243	 * @brief User-defined type reference: struct/union/enum/typedef name
   244	 */
   245	DEF_TYPE("type_identifier", TYPE_REFERENCE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   246	
   247	/**
