{"result":"     1	/**
     2	 * @file go_types.def
     3	 * @brief Go language node type mappings for AST semantic extraction
     4	 *
     5	 * @details This file defines how tree-sitter Go AST nodes are mapped to
     6	 * universal semantic types, along with strategies for extracting names and
     7	 * native context from the parse tree.
     8	 *
     9	 * ## DEF_TYPE Macro Format
    10	 *
    11	 * ```cpp
    12	 * DEF_TYPE(raw_type, semantic_type, name_extraction, native_extraction, flags)
    13	 * ```
    14	 *
    15	 * @param raw_type The tree-sitter node type string (e.g., "function_declaration")
    16	 * @param semantic_type Universal semantic classification with optional refinement bits
    17	 * @param name_extraction Strategy for extracting the node's name (see ExtractionStrategy)
    18	 * @param native_extraction Strategy for rich context extraction (see NativeExtractionStrategy)
    19	 * @param flags Behavioral flags (e.g., IS_KEYWORD, IS_SYNTAX_ONLY)
    20	 *
    21	 * ## Semantic Type Encoding
    22	 *
    23	 * Semantic types are 8-bit values where:
    24	 * - Bits 7-2: Base semantic category (e.g., DEFINITION_FUNCTION = 0x04)
    25	 * - Bits 1-0: Refinement within category (e.g., Function::LAMBDA = 0x01)
    26	 *
    27	 * Combined using bitwise OR:
    28	 * ```cpp
    29	 * DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::LAMBDA  // = 0x05
    30	 * ```
    31	 *
    32	 * ## Go Language Characteristics
    33	 *
    34	 * Go has several unique features that affect AST mapping:
    35	 *
    36	 * - **Package system**: Every file belongs to a package; `main` package is entry point
    37	 * - **Goroutines**: Lightweight threads via `go` keyword (FLOW_SYNC)
    38	 * - **Channels**: First-class communication primitives (`chan`, `<-`)
    39	 * - **Defer**: Deferred execution until function returns (FLOW_SYNC)
    40	 * - **Interfaces**: Implicit implementation (no `implements` keyword), use ABSTRACT
    41	 * - **No classes**: Only structs with methods; methods have receiver parameters
    42	 * - **Short declarations**: `:=` for declare-and-assign in one step
    43	 * - **Multiple returns**: Functions can return multiple values
    44	 * - **Range iteration**: `for range` for iterating collections
    45	 * - **Select**: Multiplexing channel operations
    46	 * - **Type switches**: Switch on type rather than value
    47	 * - **iota**: Auto-incrementing constant generator
    48	 * - **No generics keywords**: Uses `[T any]` syntax (Go 1.18+)
    49	 *
    50	 * @see node_config.hpp for ExtractionStrategy and NativeExtractionStrategy definitions
    51	 * @see semantic_types.hpp for full semantic type enumeration
    52	 */
    53	
    54	// =============================================================================
    55	// PACKAGE AND IMPORTS
    56	// =============================================================================
    57	
    58	/**
    59	 * @defgroup go_packages Package and Imports
    60	 * @brief Package declarations and import statements
    61	 *
    62	 * Every Go file starts with a package clause. The `main` package is special
    63	 * as it defines an executable. Imports bring in other packages.
    64	 *
    65	 * Note: `source_file` represents the entire file as a module.
    66	 * @{
    67	 */
    68	DEF_TYPE("source_file", DEFINITION_MODULE, NONE, NONE, 0)
    69	DEF_TYPE("package_clause", DEFINITION_MODULE, CUSTOM, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
    70	DEF_TYPE("package_identifier", NAME_IDENTIFIER, NODE_TEXT, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
    71	DEF_TYPE("import_declaration", EXTERNAL_IMPORT | SemanticRefinements::Import::MODULE, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
    72	DEF_TYPE("import_spec", EXTERNAL_IMPORT | SemanticRefinements::Import::MODULE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
    73	DEF_TYPE("import_spec_list", ORGANIZATION_LIST | SemanticRefinements::Organization::COLLECTION, NONE, NONE, 0)
    74	/** @} */
    75	
    76	// =============================================================================
    77	// FUNCTION DEFINITIONS
    78	// =============================================================================
    79	
    80	/**
    81	 * @defgroup go_functions Function Definitions
    82	 * @brief Functions, methods, and function literals
    83	 *
    84	 * Go distinguishes between:
    85	 * - `function_declaration`: Package-level functions
    86	 * - `method_declaration`: Functions with a receiver (attached to a type)
    87	 * - `func_literal`: Anonymous functions/closures
    88	 *
    89	 * Go functions always have bodies (no forward declarations).
    90	 * Methods use a receiver parameter to associate with a type.
    91	 * @{
    92	 */
    93	DEF_TYPE("function_declaration", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, FUNCTION_WITH_PARAMS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
    94	DEF_TYPE("method_declaration", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, FUNCTION_WITH_PARAMS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
    95	DEF_TYPE("func_literal", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::LAMBDA, FIND_ASSIGNMENT_TARGET, ARROW_FUNCTION, ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
    96	DEF_TYPE("method_elem", DEFINITION_FUNCTION, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
    97	/** @} */
    98	
    99	// =============================================================================
   100	// TYPE DEFINITIONS
   101	// =============================================================================
   102	
   103	/**
   104	 * @defgroup go_types_def Type Definitions
   105	 * @brief Struct and interface definitions
   106	 *
   107	 * Go uses composition over inheritance:
   108	 * - `struct_type`: Product types with fields (REGULAR refinement)
   109	 * - `interface_type`: Abstract types defining method sets (ABSTRACT refinement)
   110	 *
   111	 * Types are typically defined with `type Name struct/interface { ... }`.
   112	 * The `type_declaration` and `type_spec` wrap the actual type definition.
   113	 * @{
   114	 */
   115	DEF_TYPE("type_declaration", EXECUTION_DECLARATION, FIND_IDENTIFIER, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   116	DEF_TYPE("type_spec", EXECUTION_DECLARATION, FIND_IDENTIFIER, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   117	DEF_TYPE("struct_type", DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::REGULAR, NONE, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   118	DEF_TYPE("interface_type", DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::ABSTRACT, NONE, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   119	/** @} */
   120	
   121	// =============================================================================
   122	// VARIABLE DECLARATIONS
   123	// =============================================================================
   124	
   125	/**
   126	 * @defgroup go_variables Variable Declarations
   127	 * @brief Variable and constant declarations
   128	 *
   129	 * Go has several variable declaration forms:
   130	 * - `var x int`: Explicit var declaration (MUTABLE)
   131	 * - `x := value`: Short declaration (MUTABLE, infers type)
   132	 * - `const x = value`: Constant declaration (IMMUTABLE)
   133	 *
   134	 * The `_spec` nodes contain the actual identifier and type information.
   135	 * @{
   136	 */
   137	DEF_TYPE("var_declaration", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::MUTABLE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   138	DEF_TYPE("var_spec", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::MUTABLE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   139	DEF_TYPE("var_spec_list", ORGANIZATION_LIST | SemanticRefinements::Organization::COLLECTION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   140	DEF_TYPE("const_declaration", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::IMMUTABLE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   141	DEF_TYPE("const_spec", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::IMMUTABLE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   142	DEF_TYPE("short_var_declaration", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::MUTABLE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   143	DEF_TYPE("parameter_declaration", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::PARAMETER, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   144	DEF_TYPE("variadic_parameter_declaration", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::PARAMETER, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   145	DEF_TYPE("field_declaration", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::FIELD, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   146	/** @} */
   147	
   148	// =============================================================================
   149	// FUNCTION CALLS AND ACCESS
   150	// =============================================================================
   151	
   152	/**
   153	 * @defgroup go_calls Function Calls and Access
   154	 * @brief Function calls, method calls, and field/index access
   155	 *
   156	 * Go uses `selector_expression` for both field access and method calls.
   157	 * The FIND_PROPERTY strategy extracts the accessed member name.
   158	 *
   159	 * Type conversions in Go look like function calls: `int(x)`, `string(bytes)`.
   160	 * @{
   161	 */
   162	DEF_TYPE("call_expression", COMPUTATION_CALL | SemanticRefinements::Call::FUNCTION, FIND_CALL_TARGET, FUNCTION_CALL, 0)
   163	DEF_TYPE("type_conversion_expression", COMPUTATION_CALL, FIND_CALL_TARGET, FUNCTION_CALL, 0)
   164	DEF_TYPE("selector_expression", COMPUTATION_ACCESS, FIND_PROPERTY, NONE, 0)
   165	DEF_TYPE("index_expression", COMPUTATION_ACCESS, NONE, NONE, 0)
   166	DEF_TYPE("slice_expression", COMPUTATION_ACCESS, NONE, NONE, 0)
   167	/** @} */
   168	
   169	// =============================================================================
   170	// IDENTIFIERS
   171	// =============================================================================
   172	
   173	/**
   174	 * @defgroup go_identifiers Identifiers
   175	 * @brief Identifier nodes for values, types, and fields
   176	 *
   177	 * Go has distinct identifier types:
   178	 * - `identifier`: General value identifiers
   179	 * - `field_identifier`: Struct field names in selectors
   180	 * - `type_identifier`: Type names
   181	 * - `label_name`: Labels for goto/break/continue
   182	 * @{
   183	 */
   184	DEF_TYPE("identifier", NAME_IDENTIFIER, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   185	DEF_TYPE("field_identifier", NAME_IDENTIFIER, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   186	DEF_TYPE("type_identifier", TYPE_REFERENCE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   187	DEF_TYPE("label_name", NAME_IDENTIFIER, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   188	/** @} */
   189	
   190	// =============================================================================
   191	// LITERALS
   192	// =============================================================================
   193	
   194	/**
   195	 * @defgroup go_literals Literal Values
   196	 * @brief Numeric, string, and composite literals
   197	 *
   198	 * Go literals include:
   199	 * - Numeric: int, float, imaginary (complex numbers!)
   200	 * - String: interpreted (`"..."`) and raw (`` `...` ``)
   201	 * - Rune: single characters (`'a'`)
   202	 * - Composite: struct/array/slice/map literals
   203	 *
   204	 * The `iota` keyword is a special constant generator in const blocks.
   205	 * @{
   206	 */
   207	
   208	// Numeric literals
   209	DEF_TYPE("int_literal", LITERAL_NUMBER | SemanticRefinements::Number::INTEGER, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   210	DEF_TYPE("float_literal", LITERAL_NUMBER | SemanticRefinements::Number::FLOAT, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   211	DEF_TYPE("imaginary_literal", LITERAL_NUMBER | SemanticRefinements::Number::COMPLEX, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   212	
   213	// String literals
   214	DEF_TYPE("interpreted_string_literal", LITERAL_STRING | SemanticRefinements::String::LITERAL, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   215	DEF_TYPE("raw_string_literal", LITERAL_STRING | SemanticRefinements::String::RAW, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   216	DEF_TYPE("rune_literal", LITERAL_STRING | SemanticRefinements::String::LITERAL, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   217	DEF_TYPE("interpreted_string_literal_content", LITERAL_STRING, NONE, NONE, 0)
   218	DEF_TYPE("escape_sequence", LITERAL_STRING, NONE, NONE, 0)
   219	
   220	// Atomic literals
   221	DEF_TYPE("nil", LITERAL_ATOMIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   222	DEF_TYPE("true", LITERAL_ATOMIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   223	DEF_TYPE("false", LITERAL_ATOMIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   224	DEF_TYPE("iota", LITERAL_ATOMIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   225	
   226	// Composite literals
   227	DEF_TYPE("composite_literal", LITERAL_STRUCTURED | SemanticRefinements::Structured::SEQUENCE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   228	DEF_TYPE("literal_value", LITERAL_STRUCTURED | SemanticRefinements::Structured::SEQUENCE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   229	DEF_TYPE("literal_element", LITERAL_STRUCTURED | SemanticRefinements::Structured::SEQUENCE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   230	/** @} */
   231	
   232	// =============================================================================
   233	// CONTROL FLOW
   234	// =============================================================================
   235	
   236	/**
   237	 * @defgroup go_control Control Flow
   238	 * @brief Conditionals, loops, and jump statements
   239	 *
   240	 * Go control flow:
   241	 * - `if`: Binary conditional (can include init statement)
   242	 * - `for`: The only loop construct (counter, condition, or range-based)
   243	 * - `switch`: Value-based multiway branching
   244	 * - `type_switch`: Switch on type (unique to Go)
   245	 * - `select`: Multiplex channel operations
   246	 *
   247	 * Loop refinements:
   248	 * - CONDITIONAL: Basic `for` or `for condition`
   249	 * - ITERATOR: `for range` iteration
   250	 * @{
   251	 */
   252	
   253	// Conditional statements
   254	DEF_TYPE("if_statement", FLOW_CONDITIONAL | SemanticRefinements::Conditional::BINARY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   255	DEF_TYPE("switch_statement", FLOW_CONDITIONAL | SemanticRefinements::Conditional::MULTIWAY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   256	DEF_TYPE("expression_switch_statement", FLOW_CONDITIONAL, NONE, NONE, 0)
   257	DEF_TYPE("type_switch_statement", FLOW_CONDITIONAL | SemanticRefinements::Conditional::MULTIWAY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   258	DEF_TYPE("select_statement", FLOW_CONDITIONAL | SemanticRefinements::Conditional::MULTIWAY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   259	DEF_TYPE("expression_case", FLOW_CONDITIONAL, NONE, NONE, 0)
   260	DEF_TYPE("default_case", FLOW_CONDITIONAL, NONE, NONE, 0)
   261	
   262	// Loop statements
   263	DEF_TYPE("for_statement", FLOW_LOOP | SemanticRefinements::Loop::CONDITIONAL, NONE, NONE, 0)
   264	DEF_TYPE("for_clause", FLOW_LOOP, NONE, NONE, 0)
   265	DEF_TYPE("range_clause", FLOW_LOOP | SemanticRefinements::Loop::ITERATOR, NONE, NONE, 0)
   266	
   267	// Jump statements
   268	DEF_TYPE("return_statement", FLOW_JUMP | SemanticRefinements::Jump::RETURN, NONE, NONE, 0)
   269	DEF_TYPE("break_statement", FLOW_JUMP | SemanticRefinements::Jump::BREAK, NONE, NONE, 0)
   270	DEF_TYPE("continue_statement", FLOW_JUMP | SemanticRefinements::Jump::CONTINUE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   271	DEF_TYPE("goto_statement", FLOW_JUMP | SemanticRefinements::Jump::GOTO, NONE, NONE, 0)
   272	/** @} */
   273	
   274	// =============================================================================
   275	// CONCURRENCY (Go-Specific)
   276	// =============================================================================
   277	
   278	/**
   279	 * @defgroup go_concurrency Concurrency Constructs
   280	 * @brief Goroutines, channels, and defer
   281	 *
   282	 * Go's concurrency primitives:
   283	 * - `go_statement`: Launch a goroutine (lightweight thread)
   284	 * - `defer_statement`: Delay execution until function returns
   285	 * - `send_statement`: Send value to channel (`ch <- value`)
   286	 * - `receive_statement`: Receive from channel (`<-ch`)
   287	 * - `channel_type`: Channel type declaration (`chan T`, `<-chan T`, `chan<- T`)
   288	 *
   289	 * These use FLOW_SYNC semantic type as they relate to synchronization.
   290	 * @{
   291	 */
   292	DEF_TYPE("go_statement", FLOW_SYNC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   293	DEF_TYPE("defer_statement", FLOW_SYNC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   294	DEF_TYPE("send_statement", EXECUTION_STATEMENT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   295	DEF_TYPE("receive_statement", EXECUTION_STATEMENT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   296	DEF_TYPE("channel_type", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   297	/** @} */
   298	
   299	// =============================================================================
   300	// TYPE SYSTEM
   301	// =============================================================================
   302	
   303	/**
   304	 * @defgroup go_type_system Type System
   305	 * @brief Type references, composite types, and generics
   306	 *
   307	 * Go types include:
   308	 * - `pointer_type`: Pointer to type (`*T`)
   309	 * - `slice_type`: Dynamic array (`[]T`)
   310	 * - `array_type`: Fixed-size array (`[N]T`)
   311	 * - `map_type`: Hash map (`map[K]V`)
   312	 * - `function_type`: Function signature
   313	 * - `channel_type`: Channel (`chan T`)
   314	 *
   315	 * Go 1.18+ generics use `type_arguments` and `type_instantiation_expression`.
   316	 * @{
   317	 */
   318	
   319	// Basic type references
   320	DEF_TYPE("pointer_type", TYPE_REFERENCE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   321	DEF_TYPE("qualified_type", TYPE_REFERENCE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   322	DEF_TYPE("parenthesized_type", TYPE_REFERENCE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   323	DEF_TYPE("type_assertion_expression", TYPE_REFERENCE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   324	DEF_TYPE("type_elem", TYPE_REFERENCE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   325	
   326	// Composite types
   327	DEF_TYPE("slice_type", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   328	DEF_TYPE("array_type", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   329	DEF_TYPE("map_type", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   330	DEF_TYPE("function_type", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   331	
   332	// Generics (Go 1.18+)
   333	DEF_TYPE("generic_type", TYPE_GENERIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   334	DEF_TYPE("type_arguments", TYPE_GENERIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   335	DEF_TYPE("type_instantiation_expression", TYPE_GENERIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   336	/** @} */
   337	
   338	// =============================================================================
   339	// OPERATORS AND EXPRESSIONS
   340	// =============================================================================
   341	
   342	/**
   343	 * @defgroup go_operators Operators and Expressions
   344	 * @brief Arithmetic, logical, comparison, and assignment operators
   345	 *
   346	 * Go operators:
   347	 * - Standard arithmetic: `+`, `-`, `*`, `/`, `%`
   348	 * - Bitwise: `&`, `|`, `^`, `<<`, `>>`, `&^` (bit clear)
   349	 * - Logical: `&&`, `||`, `!`
   350	 * - Comparison: `==`, `!=`, `<`, `<=`, `>`, `>=`
   351	 * - Assignment: `=`, `:=`, `+=`, `-=`, etc.
   352	 * - Increment/decrement: `++`, `--` (statements, not expressions!)
   353	 *
   354	 * Note: `++` and `--` are statements in Go, not expressions.
   355	 * @{
   356	 */
   357	DEF_TYPE("binary_expression", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC | SemanticRefinements::Arithmetic::BINARY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   358	DEF_TYPE("unary_expression", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC | SemanticRefinements::Arithmetic::UNARY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   359	DEF_TYPE("assignment_statement", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT | SemanticRefinements::Assignment::SIMPLE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   360	DEF_TYPE("parenthesized_expression", COMPUTATION_EXPRESSION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   361	/** @} */
   362	
   363	// =============================================================================
   364	// STATEMENTS
   365	// =============================================================================
   366	
   367	/**
   368	 * @defgroup go_statements Statements
   369	 * @brief Expression statements and other execution constructs
   370	 * @{
   371	 */
   372	DEF_TYPE("expression_statement", EXECUTION_STATEMENT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   373	DEF_TYPE("inc_statement", EXECUTION_STATEMENT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   374	DEF_TYPE("dec_statement", EXECUTION_STATEMENT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   375	DEF_TYPE("labeled_statement", EXECUTION_STATEMENT, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   376	/** @} */
   377	
   378	// =============================================================================
   379	// STRUCTURAL ELEMENTS
   380	// =============================================================================
   381	
   382	/**
   383	 * @defgroup go_structure Structural Elements
   384	 * @brief Blocks, lists, and organizational nodes
   385	 * @{
   386	 */
   387	DEF_TYPE("block", ORGANIZATION_BLOCK | SemanticRefinements::Organization::SEQUENTIAL, NONE, NONE, 0)
   388	DEF_TYPE("expression_list", ORGANIZATION_LIST | SemanticRefinements::Organization::COLLECTION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   389	DEF_TYPE("argument_list", ORGANIZATION_LIST | SemanticRefinements::Organization::COLLECTION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   390	DEF_TYPE("parameter_list", ORGANIZATION_LIST | SemanticRefinements::Organization::COLLECTION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   391	DEF_TYPE("field_declaration_list", ORGANIZATION_LIST | SemanticRefinements::Organization::COLLECTION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   392	/** @} */
   393	
   394	// =============================================================================
   395	// COMMENTS
   396	// =============================================================================
   397	
   398	/// @defgroup go_comments Comments
   399	/// @brief Line and block comments
   400	///
   401	/// Go has both line comments and block comments.
   402	/// Doc comments precede declarations and use line comment style.
   403	/// @{
   404	DEF_TYPE("comment", METADATA_COMMENT, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   405	DEF_TYPE("line_comment", METADATA_COMMENT, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   406	DEF_TYPE("block_comment", METADATA_COMMENT, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   407	/** @} */
   408	
   409	// =============================================================================
   410	// PATTERNS
   411	// =============================================================================
   412	
   413	/**
   414	 * @defgroup go_patterns Patterns
   415	 * @brief Range and variadic patterns
   416	 * @{
   417	 */
   418	DEF_TYPE("range", PATTERN_COLLECT, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   419	DEF_TYPE("...", PATTERN_COLLECT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   420	/** @} */
   421	
   422	// =============================================================================
   423	// KEYWORDS
   424	// =============================================================================
   425	
   426	/**
   427	 * @defgroup go_keywords Keywords
   428	 * @brief Go reserved words as syntax tokens
   429	 *
   430	 * Keywords are marked with IS_KEYWORD flag and get the same semantic type
   431	 * as the constructs they introduce. This enables semantic queries that include
   432	 * or exclude keyword tokens as needed.
   433	 * @{
   434	 */
   435	
   436	// Package and import keywords
   437	DEF_TYPE("package", DEFINITION_MODULE, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   438	DEF_TYPE("import", EXTERNAL_IMPORT, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   439	
   440	// Definition keywords
   441	DEF_TYPE("func", DEFINITION_FUNCTION, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   442	DEF_TYPE("var", DEFINITION_VARIABLE, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   443	DEF_TYPE("const", DEFINITION_VARIABLE, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   444	DEF_TYPE("type", TYPE_REFERENCE, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   445	DEF_TYPE("struct", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   446	DEF_TYPE("interface", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   447	DEF_TYPE("map", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   448	DEF_TYPE("chan", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   449	
   450	// Control flow keywords
   451	DEF_TYPE("if", FLOW_CONDITIONAL, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   452	DEF_TYPE("else", FLOW_CONDITIONAL, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   453	DEF_TYPE("for", FLOW_LOOP, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   454	DEF_TYPE("switch", FLOW_CONDITIONAL, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   455	DEF_TYPE("case", FLOW_CONDITIONAL, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   456	DEF_TYPE("default", FLOW_CONDITIONAL, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   457	DEF_TYPE("select", FLOW_CONDITIONAL, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   458	
   459	// Jump keywords
   460	DEF_TYPE("return", FLOW_JUMP, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   461	DEF_TYPE("break", FLOW_JUMP, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   462	DEF_TYPE("continue", FLOW_JUMP, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   463	DEF_TYPE("goto", FLOW_JUMP, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   464	
   465	// Concurrency keywords
   466	DEF_TYPE("go", FLOW_SYNC, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   467	DEF_TYPE("defer", FLOW_SYNC, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   468	/** @} */
   469	
   470	// =============================================================================
   471	// OPERATORS
   472	// =============================================================================
   473	
   474	/**
   475	 * @defgroup go_operators_tokens Operator Tokens
   476	 * @brief Individual operator symbols
   477	 * @{
   478	 */
   479	
   480	// Assignment operators
   481	DEF_TYPE("=", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   482	DEF_TYPE(":=", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   483	DEF_TYPE("+=", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   484	DEF_TYPE("-=", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   485	DEF_TYPE("*=", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   486	DEF_TYPE("/=", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   487	DEF_TYPE("|=", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   488	DEF_TYPE("&^=", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   489	
   490	// Arithmetic operators
   491	DEF_TYPE("+", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   492	DEF_TYPE("-", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   493	DEF_TYPE("*", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   494	DEF_TYPE("/", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   495	DEF_TYPE("%", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   496	DEF_TYPE("&", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   497	DEF_TYPE("|", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   498	DEF_TYPE("^", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   499	DEF_TYPE("<<", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   500	DEF_TYPE(">>", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   501	DEF_TYPE("&^", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   502	DEF_TYPE("++", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   503	DEF_TYPE("--", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   504	
   505	// Comparison operators
   506	DEF_TYPE("==", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NONE, NONE, 0)
   507	DEF_TYPE("!=", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NONE, NONE, 0)
   508	DEF_TYPE("<", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NONE, NONE, 0)
   509	DEF_TYPE("<=", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NONE, NONE, 0)
   510	DEF_TYPE(">", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NONE, NONE, 0)
   511	DEF_TYPE(">=", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NONE, NONE, 0)
   512	
   513	// Logical operators
   514	DEF_TYPE("&&", OPERATOR_LOGICAL, NONE, NONE, 0)
   515	DEF_TYPE("||", OPERATOR_LOGICAL, NONE, NONE, 0)
   516	DEF_TYPE("!", OPERATOR_LOGICAL, NONE, NONE, 0)
   517	/** @} */
   518	
   519	// =============================================================================
   520	// PUNCTUATION AND DELIMITERS
   521	// =============================================================================
   522	
   523	/**
   524	 * @defgroup go_punctuation Punctuation
   525	 * @brief Delimiters, separators, and syntax markers
   526	 * @{
   527	 */
   528	DEF_TYPE("(", PARSER_DELIMITER, NONE, NONE, 0)
   529	DEF_TYPE(")", PARSER_DELIMITER, NONE, NONE, 0)
   530	DEF_TYPE("{", PARSER_DELIMITER, NONE, NONE, 0)
   531	DEF_TYPE("}", PARSER_DELIMITER, NONE, NONE, 0)
   532	DEF_TYPE("[", PARSER_DELIMITER, NONE, NONE, 0)
   533	DEF_TYPE("]", PARSER_DELIMITER, NONE, NONE, 0)
   534	DEF_TYPE(",", PARSER_PUNCTUATION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   535	DEF_TYPE(".", PARSER_PUNCTUATION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   536	DEF_TYPE(";", PARSER_PUNCTUATION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   537	DEF_TYPE(":", PARSER_PUNCTUATION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   538	DEF_TYPE("\"", PARSER_DELIMITER, NONE, NONE, 0)
   539	DEF_TYPE("dot", PARSER_PUNCTUATION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   540	/** @} */
   541	
   542	// =============================================================================
   543	// ERROR RECOVERY
   544	// =============================================================================
   545	
   546	/**
   547	 * @defgroup go_errors Parse Errors
   548	 * @brief Error nodes from failed parsing
   549	 * @{
   550	 */
   551	DEF_TYPE("ERROR", PARSER_SYNTAX, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   552	/** @} */
"}

{"result":"     1	/**
     2	 * @file go_types.def
     3	 * @brief Go language node type mappings for AST semantic extraction
     4	 *
     5	 * @details This file defines how tree-sitter Go AST nodes are mapped to
     6	 * universal semantic types, along with strategies for extracting names and
     7	 * native context from the parse tree.
     8	 *
     9	 * ## DEF_TYPE Macro Format
    10	 *
    11	 * ```cpp
    12	 * DEF_TYPE(raw_type, semantic_type, name_extraction, native_extraction, flags)
    13	 * ```
    14	 *
    15	 * @param raw_type The tree-sitter node type string (e.g., "function_declaration")
    16	 * @param semantic_type Universal semantic classification with optional refinement bits
    17	 * @param name_extraction Strategy for extracting the node's name (see ExtractionStrategy)
    18	 * @param native_extraction Strategy for rich context extraction (see NativeExtractionStrategy)
    19	 * @param flags Behavioral flags (e.g., IS_KEYWORD, IS_SYNTAX_ONLY)
    20	 *
    21	 * ## Semantic Type Encoding
    22	 *
    23	 * Semantic types are 8-bit values where:
    24	 * - Bits 7-2: Base semantic category (e.g., DEFINITION_FUNCTION = 0x04)
    25	 * - Bits 1-0: Refinement within category (e.g., Function::LAMBDA = 0x01)
    26	 *
    27	 * Combined using bitwise OR:
    28	 * ```cpp
    29	 * DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::LAMBDA  // = 0x05
    30	 * ```
    31	 *
    32	 * ## Go Language Characteristics
    33	 *
    34	 * Go has several unique features that affect AST mapping:
    35	 *
    36	 * - **Package system**: Every file belongs to a package; `main` package is entry point
    37	 * - **Goroutines**: Lightweight threads via `go` keyword (FLOW_SYNC)
    38	 * - **Channels**: First-class communication primitives (`chan`, `<-`)
    39	 * - **Defer**: Deferred execution until function returns (FLOW_SYNC)
    40	 * - **Interfaces**: Implicit implementation (no `implements` keyword), use ABSTRACT
    41	 * - **No classes**: Only structs with methods; methods have receiver parameters
    42	 * - **Short declarations**: `:=` for declare-and-assign in one step
    43	 * - **Multiple returns**: Functions can return multiple values
    44	 * - **Range iteration**: `for range` for iterating collections
    45	 * - **Select**: Multiplexing channel operations
    46	 * - **Type switches**: Switch on type rather than value
    47	 * - **iota**: Auto-incrementing constant generator
    48	 * - **No generics keywords**: Uses `[T any]` syntax (Go 1.18+)
    49	 *
    50	 * @see node_config.hpp for ExtractionStrategy and NativeExtractionStrategy definitions
    51	 * @see semantic_types.hpp for full semantic type enumeration
    52	 */
    53

    55	// PACKAGE AND IMPORTS

    58	/**
    59	 * @defgroup go_packages Package and Imports
    60	 * @brief Package declarations and import statements
    61	 *
    62	 * Every Go file starts with a package clause. The `main` package is special
    63	 * as it defines an executable. Imports bring in other packages.
    64	 *
    65	 * Note: `source_file` represents the entire file as a module.
    66	 * @{
    67	 */
    68	DEF_TYPE("source_file", DEFINITION_MODULE, NONE, NONE, 0)
    69	DEF_TYPE("package_clause", DEFINITION_MODULE, CUSTOM, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
    70	DEF_TYPE("package_identifier", NAME_IDENTIFIER, NODE_TEXT, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
    71	DEF_TYPE("import_declaration", EXTERNAL_IMPORT | SemanticRefinements::Import::MODULE, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
    72	DEF_TYPE("import_spec", EXTERNAL_IMPORT | SemanticRefinements::Import::MODULE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
    73	DEF_TYPE("import_spec_list", ORGANIZATION_LIST | SemanticRefinements::Organization::COLLECTION, NONE, NONE, 0)
    74	/** @} */

    77	// FUNCTION DEFINITIONS
    78	// =============================================================================
    79	
    80	/**
    81	 * @defgroup go_functions Function Definitions
    82	 * @brief Functions, methods, and function literals
    83	 *
    84	 * Go distinguishes between:
    85	 * - `function_declaration`: Package-level functions
    86	 * - `method_declaration`: Functions with a receiver (attached to a type)
    87	 * - `func_literal`: Anonymous functions/closures
    88	 *
    89	 * Go functions always have bodies (no forward declarations).
    90	 * Methods use a receiver parameter to associate with a type.
    91	 * @{
    92	 */
    93	DEF_TYPE("function_declaration", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, FUNCTION_WITH_PARAMS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
    94	DEF_TYPE("method_declaration", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, FUNCTION_WITH_PARAMS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
    95	DEF_TYPE("func_literal", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::LAMBDA, FIND_ASSIGNMENT_TARGET, ARROW_FUNCTION, ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
    96	DEF_TYPE("method_elem", DEFINITION_FUNCTION, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
    97	/** @} */

   102	
   103	/**
   104	 * @defgroup go_types_def Type Definitions
   105	 * @brief Struct and interface definitions
   106	 *
   107	 * Go uses composition over inheritance:
   108	 * - `struct_type`: Product types with fields (REGULAR refinement)
   109	 * - `interface_type`: Abstract types defining method sets (ABSTRACT refinement)
   110	 *
   111	 * Types are typically defined with `type Name struct/interface { ... }`.
   112	 * The `type_declaration` and `type_spec` wrap the actual type definition.
   113	 * @{
   114	 */
   115	DEF_TYPE("type_declaration", EXECUTION_DECLARATION, FIND_IDENTIFIER, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   116	DEF_TYPE("type_spec", EXECUTION_DECLARATION, FIND_IDENTIFIER, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   117	DEF_TYPE("struct_type", DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::REGULAR, NONE, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   118	DEF_TYPE("interface_type", DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::ABSTRACT, NONE, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   119	/** @} */

   236	/**
   237	 * @defgroup go_control Control Flow
   238	 * @brief Conditionals, loops, and jump statements
   239	 *
   240	 * Go control flow:
   241	 * - `if`: Binary conditional (can include init statement)
   242	 * - `for`: The only loop construct (counter, condition, or range-based)
   243	 * - `switch`: Value-based multiway branching
   244	 * - `type_switch`: Switch on type (unique to Go)
   245	 * - `select`: Multiplex channel operations
   246	 *
   247	 * Loop refinements:
   248	 * - CONDITIONAL: Basic `for` or `for condition`
   249	 * - ITERATOR: `for range` iteration
   250	 * @{
   251	 */

   256	DEF_TYPE("expression_switch_statement", FLOW_CONDITIONAL, NONE, NONE, 0)
   257	DEF_TYPE("type_switch_statement", FLOW_CONDITIONAL | SemanticRefinements::Conditional::MULTIWAY, NONE, NONE, 0)

   266

   278	/**
   279	 * @defgroup go_concurrency Concurrency Constructs

   281	 *
   282	 * Go's concurrency primitives:
   283	 * - `go_statement`: Launch a goroutine (lightweight thread)
   284	 * - `defer_statement`: Delay execution until function returns
   285	 * - `send_statement`: Send value to channel (`ch <- value`)

   295	DEF_TYPE("receive_statement", EXECUTION_STATEMENT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   296	DEF_TYPE("channel_type", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)

   492	DEF_TYPE("-", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   493	DEF_TYPE("*", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   494	DEF_TYPE("/", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   495	DEF_TYPE("%", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   496	DEF_TYPE("&", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   497	DEF_TYPE("|", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   498	DEF_TYPE("^", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   499	DEF_TYPE("<<", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   500	DEF_TYPE(">>", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   501	DEF_TYPE("&^", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   502	DEF_TYPE("++", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   503	DEF_TYPE("--", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   504	
   505	// Comparison operators
   506	DEF_TYPE("==", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NONE, NONE, 0)
   507	DEF_TYPE("!=", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NONE, NONE, 0)
   508	DEF_TYPE("<", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NONE, NONE, 0)
   509	DEF_TYPE("<=", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NONE, NONE, 0)
   510	DEF_TYPE(">", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NONE, NONE, 0)
   511	DEF_TYPE(">=", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NONE, NONE, 0)
   512	
   513	// Logical operators
   514	DEF_TYPE("&&", OPERATOR_LOGICAL, NONE, NONE, 0)
   515	DEF_TYPE("||", OPERATOR_LOGICAL, NONE, NONE, 0)
   516	DEF_TYPE("!", OPERATOR_LOGICAL, NONE, NONE, 0)
   517	/** @} */
   518	
   519	// =============================================================================
   520	// PUNCTUATION AND DELIMITERS
   521	// =============================================================================
   522	
   523	/**
   524	 * @defgroup go_punctuation Punctuation
   525	 * @brief Delimiters, separators, and syntax markers
   526	 * @{
   527	 */
   528	DEF_TYPE("(", PARSER_DELIMITER, NONE, NONE, 0)
   529	DEF_TYPE(")", PARSER_DELIMITER, NONE, NONE, 0)
   530	DEF_TYPE("{", PARSER_DELIMITER, NONE, NONE, 0)
   531	DEF_TYPE("}", PARSER_DELIMITER, NONE, NONE, 0)
   532	DEF_TYPE("[", PARSER_DELIMITER, NONE, NONE, 0)
   533	DEF_TYPE("]", PARSER_DELIMITER, NONE, NONE, 0)
   534	DEF_TYPE(",", PARSER_PUNCTUATION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   535	DEF_TYPE(".", PARSER_PUNCTUATION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   536	DEF_TYPE(";", PARSER_PUNCTUATION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   537	DEF_TYPE(":", PARSER_PUNCTUATION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   538	DEF_TYPE("\"", PARSER_DELIMITER, NONE, NONE, 0)
   539	DEF_TYPE("dot", PARSER_PUNCTUATION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   540	/** @} */
   541	
   542	// =============================================================================
   543	// ERROR RECOVERY
   544	// =============================================================================
   545	
   546	/**
   547	 * @defgroup go_errors Parse Errors
   548	 * @brief Error nodes from failed parsing
   549	 * @{
   550	 */
   551	DEF_TYPE("ERROR", PARSER_SYNTAX, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   552	/** @} */
"}

1	/**
     2	 * @file go_types.def
     3	 * @brief Go language node type mappings for AST semantic extraction
     4	 *
     5	 * @details This file defines how tree-sitter Go AST nodes are mapped to
     6	 * universal semantic types, along with strategies for extracting names and
     7	 * native context from the parse tree.
     8	 *
     9	 * ## DEF_TYPE Macro Format
    10	 *
    11	 * ```cpp
    12	 * DEF_TYPE(raw_type, semantic_type, name_extraction, native_extraction, flags)
    13	 * ```
    14	 *
    15	 * @param raw_type The tree-sitter node type string (e.g., "function_declaration")
    16	 * @param semantic_type Universal semantic classification with optional refinement bits
    17	 * @param name_extraction Strategy for extracting the node's name (see ExtractionStrategy)
    18	 * @param native_extraction Strategy for rich context extraction (see NativeExtractionStrategy)
    19	 * @param flags Behavioral flags (e.g., IS_KEYWORD, IS_SYNTAX_ONLY)
    20	 *
    21	 * ## Semantic Type Encoding
    22	 *
    23	 * Semantic types are 8-bit values where:
    24	 * - Bits 7-2: Base semantic category (e.g., DEFINITION_FUNCTION = 0x04)
    25	 * - Bits 1-0: Refinement within category (e.g., Function::LAMBDA = 0x01)
    26	 *
    27	 * Combined using bitwise OR:
    28	 * ```cpp
    29	 * DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::LAMBDA  // = 0x05
    30	 * ```
    31	 *
    32	 * ## Go Language Characteristics
    33	 *
    34	 * Go has several unique features that affect AST mapping:
    35	 *
    36	 * - **Package system**: Every file belongs to a package; `main` package is entry point
    37	 * - **Goroutines**: Lightweight threads via `go` keyword (FLOW_SYNC)
    38	 * - **Channels**: First-class communication primitives (`chan`, `<-`)
    39	 * - **Defer**: Deferred execution until function returns (FLOW_SYNC)
    40	 * - **Interfaces**: Implicit implementation (no `implements` keyword), use ABSTRACT
    41	 * - **No classes**: Only structs with methods; methods have receiver parameters
    42	 * - **Short declarations**: `:=` for declare-and-assign in one step
    43	 * - **Multiple returns**: Functions can return multiple values
    44	 * - **Range iteration**: `for range` for iterating collections
    45	 * - **Select**: Multiplexing channel operations
    46	 * - **Type switches**: Switch on type rather than value
    47	 * - **iota**: Auto-incrementing constant generator
    48	 * - **No generics keywords**: Uses `[T any]` syntax (Go 1.18+)
    49	 *
    50	 * @see node_config.hpp for ExtractionStrategy and NativeExtractionStrategy definitions
    51	 * @see semantic_types.hpp for full semantic type enumeration
    52	 */
    53	
    54	// =============================================================================
    55	// PACKAGE AND IMPORTS
    56	// =============================================================================
    57	
    58	/**
    59	 * @defgroup go_packages Package and Imports
    60	 * @brief Package declarations and import statements
    61	 *
    62	 * Every Go file starts with a package clause. The `main` package is special
    63	 * as it defines an executable. Imports bring in other packages.
    64	 *
    65	 * Note: `source_file` represents the entire file as a module.
    66	 * @{
    67	 */
    68	DEF_TYPE("source_file", DEFINITION_MODULE, NONE, NONE, 0)
    69	DEF_TYPE("package_clause", DEFINITION_MODULE, CUSTOM, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
    70	DEF_TYPE("package_identifier", NAME_IDENTIFIER, NODE_TEXT, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
    71	DEF_TYPE("import_declaration", EXTERNAL_IMPORT | SemanticRefinements::Import::MODULE, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
    72	DEF_TYPE("import_spec", EXTERNAL_IMPORT | SemanticRefinements::Import::MODULE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
    73	DEF_TYPE("import_spec_list", ORGANIZATION_LIST | SemanticRefinements::Organization::COLLECTION, NONE, NONE, 0)
    74	/** @} */
    75	
    76	// =============================================================================
    77	// FUNCTION DEFINITIONS
    78	// =============================================================================
    79	
    80	/**
    81	 * @defgroup go_functions Function Definitions
    82	 * @brief Functions, methods, and function literals
    83	 *
    84	 * Go distinguishes between:
    85	 * - `function_declaration`: Package-level functions
    86	 * - `method_declaration`: Functions with a receiver (attached to a type)
    87	 * - `func_literal`: Anonymous functions/closures
    88	 *
    89	 * Go functions always have bodies (no forward declarations).
    90	 * Methods use a receiver parameter to associate with a type.
    91	 * @{
    92	 */
    93	DEF_TYPE("function_declaration", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, FUNCTION_WITH_PARAMS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
    94	DEF_TYPE("method_declaration", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::REGULAR, FIND_IDENTIFIER, FUNCTION_WITH_PARAMS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT | ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
    95	DEF_TYPE("func_literal", DEFINITION_FUNCTION | SemanticRefinements::Function::LAMBDA, FIND_ASSIGNMENT_TARGET, ARROW_FUNCTION, ASTNodeFlags::IS_EMBODIED)
    96	DEF_TYPE("method_elem", DEFINITION_FUNCTION, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
    97	/** @} */
    98	
    99	// =============================================================================
   100	// TYPE DEFINITIONS
   101	// =============================================================================
   102	
   103	/**
   104	 * @defgroup go_types_def Type Definitions
   105	 * @brief Struct and interface definitions
   106	 *
   107	 * Go uses composition over inheritance:
   108	 * - `struct_type`: Product types with fields (REGULAR refinement)
   109	 * - `interface_type`: Abstract types defining method sets (ABSTRACT refinement)
   110	 *
   111	 * Types are typically defined with `type Name struct/interface { ... }`.
   112	 * The `type_declaration` and `type_spec` wrap the actual type definition.
   113	 * @{
   114	 */
   115	DEF_TYPE("type_declaration", EXECUTION_DECLARATION, FIND_IDENTIFIER, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   116	DEF_TYPE("type_spec", EXECUTION_DECLARATION, FIND_IDENTIFIER, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   117	DEF_TYPE("struct_type", DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::REGULAR, NONE, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   118	DEF_TYPE("interface_type", DEFINITION_CLASS | SemanticRefinements::Class::ABSTRACT, NONE, CLASS_WITH_METHODS, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   119	/** @} */
   120	
   121	// =============================================================================
   122	// VARIABLE DECLARATIONS
   123	// =============================================================================
   124	
   125	/**
   126	 * @defgroup go_variables Variable Declarations
   127	 * @brief Variable and constant declarations
   128	 *
   129	 * Go has several variable declaration forms:
   130	 * - `var x int`: Explicit var declaration (MUTABLE)
   131	 * - `x := value`: Short declaration (MUTABLE, infers type)
   132	 * - `const x = value`: Constant declaration (IMMUTABLE)
   133	 *
   134	 * The `_spec` nodes contain the actual identifier and type information.
   135	 * @{
   136	 */
   137	DEF_TYPE("var_declaration", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::MUTABLE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   138	DEF_TYPE("var_spec", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::MUTABLE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   139	DEF_TYPE("var_spec_list", ORGANIZATION_LIST | SemanticRefinements::Organization::COLLECTION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   140	DEF_TYPE("const_declaration", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::IMMUTABLE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   141	DEF_TYPE("const_spec", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::IMMUTABLE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   142	DEF_TYPE("short_var_declaration", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::MUTABLE, FIND_IDENTIFIER, VARIABLE_WITH_TYPE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_CONSTRUCT)
   143	DEF_TYPE("parameter_declaration", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::PARAMETER, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   144	DEF_TYPE("variadic_parameter_declaration", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::PARAMETER, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   145	DEF_TYPE("field_declaration", DEFINITION_VARIABLE | SemanticRefinements::Variable::FIELD, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   146	/** @} */
   147	
   148	// =============================================================================
   149	// FUNCTION CALLS AND ACCESS
   150	// =============================================================================
   151	
   152	/**
   153	 * @defgroup go_calls Function Calls and Access
   154	 * @brief Function calls, method calls, and field/index access
   155	 *
   156	 * Go uses `selector_expression` for both field access and method calls.
   157	 * The FIND_PROPERTY strategy extracts the accessed member name.
   158	 *
   159	 * Type conversions in Go look like function calls: `int(x)`, `string(bytes)`.
   160	 * @{
   161	 */
   162	DEF_TYPE("call_expression", COMPUTATION_CALL | SemanticRefinements::Call::FUNCTION, FIND_CALL_TARGET, FUNCTION_CALL, 0)
   163	DEF_TYPE("type_conversion_expression", COMPUTATION_CALL, FIND_CALL_TARGET, FUNCTION_CALL, 0)
   164	DEF_TYPE("selector_expression", COMPUTATION_ACCESS, FIND_PROPERTY, NONE, 0)
   165	DEF_TYPE("index_expression", COMPUTATION_ACCESS, NONE, NONE, 0)
   166	DEF_TYPE("slice_expression", COMPUTATION_ACCESS, NONE, NONE, 0)
   167	/** @} */
   168	
   169	// =============================================================================
   170	// IDENTIFIERS
   171	// =============================================================================
   172	
   173	/**
   174	 * @defgroup go_identifiers Identifiers
   175	 * @brief Identifier nodes for values, types, and fields
   176	 *
   177	 * Go has distinct identifier types:
   178	 * - `identifier`: General value identifiers
   179	 * - `field_identifier`: Struct field names in selectors
   180	 * - `type_identifier`: Type names
   181	 * - `label_name`: Labels for goto/break/continue
   182	 * @{
   183	 */
   184	DEF_TYPE("identifier", NAME_IDENTIFIER, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   185	DEF_TYPE("field_identifier", NAME_IDENTIFIER, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   186	DEF_TYPE("type_identifier", TYPE_REFERENCE, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   187	DEF_TYPE("label_name", NAME_IDENTIFIER, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   188	/** @} */
   189	
   190	// =============================================================================
   191	// LITERALS
   192	// =============================================================================
   193	
   194	/**
   195	 * @defgroup go_literals Literal Values
   196	 * @brief Numeric, string, and composite literals
   197	 *
   198	 * Go literals include:
   199	 * - Numeric: int, float, imaginary (complex numbers!)
   200	 * - String: interpreted (`"..."`) and raw (`` `...` ``)
   201	 * - Rune: single characters (`'a'`)
   202	 * - Composite: struct/array/slice/map literals
   203	 *
   204	 * The `iota` keyword is a special constant generator in const blocks.
   205	 * @{
   206	 */
   207	
   208	// Numeric literals
   209	DEF_TYPE("int_literal", LITERAL_NUMBER | SemanticRefinements::Number::INTEGER, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   210	DEF_TYPE("float_literal", LITERAL_NUMBER | SemanticRefinements::Number::FLOAT, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   211	DEF_TYPE("imaginary_literal", LITERAL_NUMBER | SemanticRefinements::Number::COMPLEX, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   212	
   213	// String literals
   214	DEF_TYPE("interpreted_string_literal", LITERAL_STRING | SemanticRefinements::String::LITERAL, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   215	DEF_TYPE("raw_string_literal", LITERAL_STRING | SemanticRefinements::String::RAW, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   216	DEF_TYPE("rune_literal", LITERAL_STRING | SemanticRefinements::String::LITERAL, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   217	DEF_TYPE("interpreted_string_literal_content", LITERAL_STRING, NONE, NONE, 0)
   218	DEF_TYPE("escape_sequence", LITERAL_STRING, NONE, NONE, 0)
   219	
   220	// Atomic literals
   221	DEF_TYPE("nil", LITERAL_ATOMIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   222	DEF_TYPE("true", LITERAL_ATOMIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   223	DEF_TYPE("false", LITERAL_ATOMIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   224	DEF_TYPE("iota", LITERAL_ATOMIC, NODE_TEXT, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   225	
   226	// Composite literals
   227	DEF_TYPE("composite_literal", LITERAL_STRUCTURED | SemanticRefinements::Structured::SEQUENCE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   228	DEF_TYPE("literal_value", LITERAL_STRUCTURED | SemanticRefinements::Structured::SEQUENCE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   229	DEF_TYPE("literal_element", LITERAL_STRUCTURED | SemanticRefinements::Structured::SEQUENCE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   230	/** @} */
   231	
   232	// =============================================================================
   233	// CONTROL FLOW
   234	// =============================================================================
   235	
   236	/**
   237	 * @defgroup go_control Control Flow
   238	 * @brief Conditionals, loops, and jump statements
   239	 *
   240	 * Go control flow:
   241	 * - `if`: Binary conditional (can include init statement)
   242	 * - `for`: The only loop construct (counter, condition, or range-based)
   243	 * - `switch`: Value-based multiway branching
   244	 * - `type_switch`: Switch on type (unique to Go)
   245	 * - `select`: Multiplex channel operations
   246	 *
   247	 * Loop refinements:
   248	 * - CONDITIONAL: Basic `for` or `for condition`
   249	 * - ITERATOR: `for range` iteration
   250	 * @{
   251	 */
   252	
   253	// Conditional statements
   254	DEF_TYPE("if_statement", FLOW_CONDITIONAL | SemanticRefinements::Conditional::BINARY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   255	DEF_TYPE("switch_statement", FLOW_CONDITIONAL | SemanticRefinements::Conditional::MULTIWAY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   256	DEF_TYPE("expression_switch_statement", FLOW_CONDITIONAL, NONE, NONE, 0)
   257	DEF_TYPE("type_switch_statement", FLOW_CONDITIONAL | SemanticRefinements::Conditional::MULTIWAY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   258	DEF_TYPE("select_statement", FLOW_CONDITIONAL | SemanticRefinements::Conditional::MULTIWAY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   259	DEF_TYPE("expression_case", FLOW_CONDITIONAL, NONE, NONE, 0)
   260	DEF_TYPE("default_case", FLOW_CONDITIONAL, NONE, NONE, 0)
   261	
   262	// Loop statements
   263	DEF_TYPE("for_statement", FLOW_LOOP | SemanticRefinements::Loop::CONDITIONAL, NONE, NONE, 0)
   264	DEF_TYPE("for_clause", FLOW_LOOP, NONE, NONE, 0)
   265	DEF_TYPE("range_clause", FLOW_LOOP | SemanticRefinements::Loop::ITERATOR, NONE, NONE, 0)
   266	
   267	// Jump statements
   268	DEF_TYPE("return_statement", FLOW_JUMP | SemanticRefinements::Jump::RETURN, NONE, NONE, 0)
   269	DEF_TYPE("break_statement", FLOW_JUMP | SemanticRefinements::Jump::BREAK, NONE, NONE, 0)
   270	DEF_TYPE("continue_statement", FLOW_JUMP | SemanticRefinements::Jump::CONTINUE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   271	DEF_TYPE("goto_statement", FLOW_JUMP | SemanticRefinements::Jump::GOTO, NONE, NONE, 0)
   272	/** @} */
   273	
   274	// =============================================================================
   275	// CONCURRENCY (Go-Specific)
   276	// =============================================================================
   277	
   278	/**
   279	 * @defgroup go_concurrency Concurrency Constructs
   280	 * @brief Goroutines, channels, and defer
   281	 *
   282	 * Go's concurrency primitives:
   283	 * - `go_statement`: Launch a goroutine (lightweight thread)
   284	 * - `defer_statement`: Delay execution until function returns
   285	 * - `send_statement`: Send value to channel (`ch <- value`)
   286	 * - `receive_statement`: Receive from channel (`<-ch`)
   287	 * - `channel_type`: Channel type declaration (`chan T`, `<-chan T`, `chan<- T`)
   288	 *
   289	 * These use FLOW_SYNC semantic type as they relate to synchronization.
   290	 * @{
   291	 */
   292	DEF_TYPE("go_statement", FLOW_SYNC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   293	DEF_TYPE("defer_statement", FLOW_SYNC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   294	DEF_TYPE("send_statement", EXECUTION_STATEMENT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   295	DEF_TYPE("receive_statement", EXECUTION_STATEMENT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   296	DEF_TYPE("channel_type", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   297	/** @} */
   298	
   299	// =============================================================================
   300	// TYPE SYSTEM
   301	// =============================================================================
   302	
   303	/**
   304	 * @defgroup go_type_system Type System
   305	 * @brief Type references, composite types, and generics
   306	 *
   307	 * Go types include:
   308	 * - `pointer_type`: Pointer to type (`*T`)
   309	 * - `slice_type`: Dynamic array (`[]T`)
   310	 * - `array_type`: Fixed-size array (`[N]T`)
   311	 * - `map_type`: Hash map (`map[K]V`)
   312	 * - `function_type`: Function signature
   313	 * - `channel_type`: Channel (`chan T`)
   314	 *
   315	 * Go 1.18+ generics use `type_arguments` and `type_instantiation_expression`.
   316	 * @{
   317	 */
   318	
   319	// Basic type references
   320	DEF_TYPE("pointer_type", TYPE_REFERENCE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   321	DEF_TYPE("qualified_type", TYPE_REFERENCE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   322	DEF_TYPE("parenthesized_type", TYPE_REFERENCE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   323	DEF_TYPE("type_assertion_expression", TYPE_REFERENCE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   324	DEF_TYPE("type_elem", TYPE_REFERENCE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   325	
   326	// Composite types
   327	DEF_TYPE("slice_type", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   328	DEF_TYPE("array_type", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   329	DEF_TYPE("map_type", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   330	DEF_TYPE("function_type", TYPE_COMPOSITE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   331	
   332	// Generics (Go 1.18+)
   333	DEF_TYPE("generic_type", TYPE_GENERIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   334	DEF_TYPE("type_arguments", TYPE_GENERIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   335	DEF_TYPE("type_instantiation_expression", TYPE_GENERIC, NONE, NONE, 0)
   336	/** @} */
   337	
   338	// =============================================================================
   339	// OPERATORS AND EXPRESSIONS
   340	// =============================================================================
   341	
   342	/**
   343	 * @defgroup go_operators Operators and Expressions
   344	 * @brief Arithmetic, logical, comparison, and assignment operators
   345	 *
   346	 * Go operators:
   347	 * - Standard arithmetic: `+`, `-`, `*`, `/`, `%`
   348	 * - Bitwise: `&`, `|`, `^`, `<<`, `>>`, `&^` (bit clear)
   349	 * - Logical: `&&`, `||`, `!`
   350	 * - Comparison: `==`, `!=`, `<`, `<=`, `>`, `>=`
   351	 * - Assignment: `=`, `:=`, `+=`, `-=`, etc.
   352	 * - Increment/decrement: `++`, `--` (statements, not expressions!)
   353	 *
   354	 * Note: `++` and `--` are statements in Go, not expressions.
   355	 * @{
   356	 */
   357	DEF_TYPE("binary_expression", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC | SemanticRefinements::Arithmetic::BINARY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   358	DEF_TYPE("unary_expression", OPERATOR_ARITHMETIC | SemanticRefinements::Arithmetic::UNARY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   359	DEF_TYPE("assignment_statement", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT | SemanticRefinements::Assignment::SIMPLE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   360	DEF_TYPE("parenthesized_expression", COMPUTATION_EXPRESSION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   361	/** @} */
   362	
   363	// =============================================================================
   364	// STATEMENTS
   365	// =============================================================================
   366	
   367	/**
   368	 * @defgroup go_statements Statements
   369	 * @brief Expression statements and other execution constructs
   370	 * @{
   371	 */
   372	DEF_TYPE("expression_statement", EXECUTION_STATEMENT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   373	DEF_TYPE("inc_statement", EXECUTION_STATEMENT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   374	DEF_TYPE("dec_statement", EXECUTION_STATEMENT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   375	DEF_TYPE("labeled_statement", EXECUTION_STATEMENT, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, 0)
   376	/** @} */
   377	
   378	// =============================================================================
   379	// STRUCTURAL ELEMENTS
   380	// =============================================================================
   381	
   382	/**
   383	 * @defgroup go_structure Structural Elements
   384	 * @brief Blocks, lists, and organizational nodes
   385	 * @{
   386	 */
   387	DEF_TYPE("block", ORGANIZATION_BLOCK | SemanticRefinements::Organization::SEQUENTIAL, NONE, NONE, 0)
   388	DEF_TYPE("expression_list", ORGANIZATION_LIST | SemanticRefinements::Organization::COLLECTION, NONE, NONE, 0)
   389	DEF_TYPE("argument_list", ORGANIZATION_LIST | SemanticRefinements::Organization::COLLECTION, NONE, NONE, 0)

   421	
   422	// =============================================================================
   423	// KEYWORDS
   424	// =============================================================================
   425	
   426	/**
   427	 * @defgroup go_keywords Keywords

   429	 *

   431	 * as the constructs they introduce. This enables semantic queries that include
   432	 * or exclude keyword tokens as needed.
   433	 * @{
   434	 */

   456	DEF_TYPE("default", FLOW_CONDITIONAL, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   457	DEF_TYPE("select", FLOW_CONDITIONAL, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   458	
   459	// Jump keywords
   460	DEF_TYPE("return", FLOW_JUMP, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   461	DEF_TYPE("break", FLOW_JUMP, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)

   463	DEF_TYPE("goto", FLOW_JUMP, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   464	
   465	// Concurrency keywords
   466	DEF_TYPE("go", FLOW_SYNC, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)

{"result":"   401	/// @brief Redo statement - restarts current iteration
   402	DEF_TYPE("redo", FLOW_JUMP | SemanticRefinements::Jump::CONTINUE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   403	
   404	/// @brief Retry statement - restarts from begin block
   405	DEF_TYPE("retry", FLOW_JUMP | SemanticRefinements::Jump::CONTINUE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   406	
   407	/** @} */ // end ruby_jumps
   408	
   409	// ============================================================================
   410	// EXCEPTION HANDLING
   411	// ============================================================================
   412	
   413	/**
   414	 * @defgroup ruby_exceptions Exception Handling
   415	 * @brief Ruby error handling constructs
   416	 *
   417	 * Ruby exception handling:
   418	 * - `begin ... rescue ... ensure ... end`
   419	 * - `raise` to throw exceptions
   420	 * - Multiple rescue clauses for different exception types
   421	 * - `retry` to re-execute begin block
   422	 * - Modifier form: `expr rescue default_value`
   423	 * @{
   424	 */
   425	
   426	/// @brief Begin block - starts exception handling region
   427	DEF_TYPE("begin", ERROR_TRY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   428	
   429	/// @brief Rescue clause - catches exceptions
   430	DEF_TYPE("rescue", ERROR_CATCH, NONE, NONE, 0)
   431	
   432	/// @brief Ensure clause - always executed (like finally)
   433	DEF_TYPE("ensure", ERROR_FINALLY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   434	
   435	/// @brief Raise statement - throws exception
   436	DEF_TYPE("raise", ERROR_THROW, NONE, NONE, 0)
   437	
   438	/** @} */ // end ruby_exceptions
   439	
   440	// ============================================================================
   441	// BLOCKS AND CLOSURES
   442	// ============================================================================
   443	
   444	/**
   445	 * @defgroup ruby_blocks Blocks and Closures
   446	 * @brief Ruby block and lambda constructs
   447	 *
   448	 * Ruby closures:
   449	 * - Blocks: `{ |params| body }` or `do |params| body end`
   450	 * - Procs: `Proc.new { }` or `proc { }`
   451	 * - Lambdas: `lambda { }` or `-> { }` (strict arity)
   452	 *
   453	 * Blocks are passed implicitly to methods:
   454	 * - `yield` calls the block
   455	 * - `block_given?` checks for block
   456	 * - `&block` captures block as Proc parameter
   457	 * @{
   458	 */
   459	
   460	/// @brief Block - `{ |params| ... }` single-line form
   461	DEF_TYPE("block", ORGANIZATION_BLOCK, NONE, ARROW_FUNCTION, 0)
   462	
   463	/// @brief Do block - `do |params| ... end` multi-line form
   464	DEF_TYPE("do_block", ORGANIZATION_BLOCK, NONE, ARROW_FUNCTION, 0)
   465	
   466	/// @brief Lambda - `-> { }` or `lambda { }`
   467	DEF_TYPE("lambda", COMPUTATION_CLOSURE, NONE, ARROW_FUNCTION, 0)
   468	
   469	/// @brief Proc - `proc { }` or `Proc.new { }`
   470	DEF_TYPE("proc", COMPUTATION_CLOSURE, NONE, ARROW_FUNCTION, 0)
   471	
   472	/** @} */ // end ruby_blocks
   473	
   474	// ============================================================================
   475	// IMPORTS
   476	// ============================================================================
   477	
   478	/**
   479	 * @defgroup ruby_imports Import Constructs
   480	 * @brief Ruby file loading mechanisms
   481	 *
   482	 * Ruby file loading:
   483	 * - `require 'lib'` - load from $LOAD_PATH (once)
   484	 * - `require_relative './file'` - load relative to current file
   485	 * - `load 'file.rb'` - always reload
   486	 * - `include Module` - mix in module methods as instance methods
   487	 * - `extend Module` - mix in module methods as class methods
   488	 * - `prepend Module` - insert module before class in method chain
   489	 * @{
   490	 */
   491	
   492	/// @brief Require - loads library from load path
   493	DEF_TYPE("require", EXTERNAL_IMPORT, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   494	
   495	/// @brief Require relative - loads file relative to current
   496	DEF_TYPE("require_relative", EXTERNAL_IMPORT, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   497	
   498	/// @brief Load - loads and re-evaluates file
   499	DEF_TYPE("load", EXTERNAL_IMPORT, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   500	
   501	/// @brief Include - mixes in module as instance methods
   502	DEF_TYPE("include", EXTERNAL_IMPORT, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   503	
   504	/// @brief Extend - mixes in module as class methods
   505	DEF_TYPE("extend", EXTERNAL_IMPORT, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   506	
   507	/// @brief Prepend - inserts module before class in method chain
   508	DEF_TYPE("prepend", EXTERNAL_IMPORT, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   509	
   510	/** @} */ // end ruby_imports
   511	
   512	// ============================================================================
   513	// COMMENTS
   514	// ============================================================================
   515	
   516	/**
   517	 * @defgroup ruby_comments Comments
   518	 * @brief Documentation and annotation
   519	 * @{
   520	 */
   521	
   522	/// @brief Comment - `# single line comment`
   523	DEF_TYPE("comment", METADATA_COMMENT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   524	
   525	/** @} */ // end ruby_comments
   526	
   527	// ============================================================================
   528	// SPECIAL CONSTRUCTS
   529	// ============================================================================
   530	
   531	/**
   532	 * @defgroup ruby_special Special Ruby Constructs
   533	 * @brief Ruby-specific language features
   534	 *
   535	 * Ruby special constructs:
   536	 * - `yield` - invoke passed block
   537	 * - `super` - call parent method
   538	 * - `self` - current object reference
   539	 * @{
   540	 */
   541

   544	
   545	/// @brief Super - calls parent class method
   546	DEF_TYPE("super", COMPUTATION_CALL, NONE, NONE, 0)
   547	
   548	/// @brief Self - reference to current object
   549	DEF_TYPE("self", NAME_SCOPED, NODE_TEXT, NONE, 0)
   550	
   551	/** @} */ // end ruby_special
   552	
   553	// ============================================================================
   554	// OPERATORS
   555	// ============================================================================
   556	
   557	/**
   558	 * @defgroup ruby_operators Operators
   559	 * @brief Ruby operators and expressions
   560	 * @{
   561	 */
   562

   712	// LOGICAL OPERATORS
   713	// ============================================================================
   714	
   715	/**
   716	 * @defgroup ruby_logical Logical Operators
   717	 * @brief Boolean operators
   718	 *
   719	 * Ruby has both symbolic and keyword logical operators:
   720	 * - `&&` / `and` - logical AND (different precedence)
   721	 * - `||` / `or` - logical OR (different precedence)
   722	 * - `!` / `not` - logical NOT
   723	 * @{
   724	 */
   725	
   726	DEF_TYPE("&&", OPERATOR_LOGICAL, NONE, NONE, 0)
   727	DEF_TYPE("||", OPERATOR_LOGICAL, NONE, NONE, 0)
   728	DEF_TYPE("!", OPERATOR_LOGICAL, NONE, NONE, 0)
   729	
   730	/** @} */ // end ruby_logical
   731	
   732	// ============================================================================
   733	// COMPARISON OPERATORS
   734	// ============================================================================
   735	
   736	/**
   737	 * @defgroup ruby_comparison Comparison Operators
   738	 * @brief Equality and ordering comparisons
   739	 *
   740	 * Ruby comparison operators:
   741	 * - `==` - equality by value
   742	 * - `===` - case equality (pattern matching)
   743	 * - `<=>` - spaceship operator (returns -1, 0, 1)
   744	 * - `=~` / `!~` - regex match/non-match
   745	 * @{
   746	 */
   747	
   748	DEF_TYPE("==", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NONE, NONE, 0)
   749	DEF_TYPE("!=", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NONE, NONE, 0)
   750	DEF_TYPE("<", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NONE, NONE, 0)
   751	DEF_TYPE(">", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NONE, NONE, 0)
   752	DEF_TYPE("<=", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NONE, NONE, 0)
   753	DEF_TYPE(">=", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NONE, NONE, 0)
   754	DEF_TYPE("<=>", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NONE, NONE, 0)
   755	DEF_TYPE("===", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NONE, NONE, 0)
   756	DEF_TYPE("=~", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NONE, NONE, 0)
   757	DEF_TYPE("!~", OPERATOR_COMPARISON, NONE, NONE, 0)
   758	
   759	/** @} */ // end ruby_comparison
   760	
   761	// ============================================================================
   762	// ASSIGNMENT OPERATORS
   763	// ============================================================================
   764	
   765	/**
   766	 * @defgroup ruby_assignment Assignment Operators
   767	 * @brief Assignment and compound assignment
   768	 *
   769	 * Notable Ruby idioms:
   770	 * - `||=` - memoization: `@cached ||= expensive_computation`
   771	 * - `&&=` - conditional assignment if truthy
   772	 * @{
   773	 */
   774	
   775	DEF_TYPE("=", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT, NONE, NONE, 0)

   777	DEF_TYPE("-=", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT, NONE, NONE, 0)

   780	DEF_TYPE("%=", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   781	DEF_TYPE("**=", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   782	DEF_TYPE("&=", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   783	DEF_TYPE("|=", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   784	DEF_TYPE("^=", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   785	DEF_TYPE("<<=", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   786	DEF_TYPE(">>=", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   787	DEF_TYPE("&&=", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   788	DEF_TYPE("||=", OPERATOR_ASSIGNMENT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   789	
   790	/** @} */ // end ruby_assignment

{"result":"   401	/// @brief Redo statement - restarts current iteration
   402	DEF_TYPE("redo", FLOW_JUMP | SemanticRefinements::Jump::CONTINUE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   403	
   404	/// @brief Retry statement - restarts from begin block
   405	DEF_TYPE("retry", FLOW_JUMP | SemanticRefinements::Jump::CONTINUE, NONE, NONE, 0)
   406	
   407	/** @} */ // end ruby_jumps
   408	
   409	// ============================================================================
   410	// EXCEPTION HANDLING
   411	// ============================================================================
   412	
   413	/**
   414	 * @defgroup ruby_exceptions Exception Handling
   415	 * @brief Ruby error handling constructs
   416	 *
   417	 * Ruby exception handling:
   418	 * - `begin ... rescue ... ensure ... end`
   419	 * - `raise` to throw exceptions
   420	 * - Multiple rescue clauses for different exception types
   421	 * - `retry` to re-execute begin block
   422	 * - Modifier form: `expr rescue default_value`
   423	 * @{
   424	 */
   425	
   426	/// @brief Begin block - starts exception handling region
   427	DEF_TYPE("begin", ERROR_TRY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   428	
   429	/// @brief Rescue clause - catches exceptions
   430	DEF_TYPE("rescue", ERROR_CATCH, NONE, NONE, 0)
   431	
   432	/// @brief Ensure clause - always executed (like finally)
   433	DEF_TYPE("ensure", ERROR_FINALLY, NONE, NONE, 0)
   434	
   435	/// @brief Raise statement - throws exception
   436	DEF_TYPE("raise", ERROR_THROW, NONE, NONE, 0)
   437	
   438	/** @} */ // end ruby_exceptions
   439	
   440	// ============================================================================
   441	// BLOCKS AND CLOSURES
   442	// ============================================================================
   443	
   444	/**
   445	 * @defgroup ruby_blocks Blocks and Closures
   446	 * @brief Ruby block and lambda constructs
   447	 *
   448	 * Ruby closures:
   449	 * - Blocks: `{ |params| body }` or `do |params| body end`
   450	 * - Procs: `Proc.new { }` or `proc { }`
   451	 * - Lambdas: `lambda { }` or `-> { }` (strict arity)
   452	 *
   453	 * Blocks are passed implicitly to methods:
   454	 * - `yield` calls the block
   455	 * - `block_given?` checks for block
   456	 * - `&block` captures block as Proc parameter
   457	 * @{
   458	 */
   459	
   460	/// @brief Block - `{ |params| ... }` single-line form
   461	DEF_TYPE("block", ORGANIZATION_BLOCK, NONE, ARROW_FUNCTION, 0)
   462	
   463	/// @brief Do block - `do |params| ... end` multi-line form
   464	DEF_TYPE("do_block", ORGANIZATION_BLOCK, NONE, ARROW_FUNCTION, 0)
   465	
   466	/// @brief Lambda - `-> { }` or `lambda { }`
   467	DEF_TYPE("lambda", COMPUTATION_CLOSURE, NONE, ARROW_FUNCTION, 0)
   468	
   469	/// @brief Proc - `proc { }` or `Proc.new { }`
   470	DEF_TYPE("proc", COMPUTATION_CLOSURE, NONE, ARROW_FUNCTION, 0)
   471	
   472	/** @} */ // end ruby_blocks
   473	
   474	// ============================================================================
   475	// IMPORTS
   476	// ============================================================================
   477	
   478	/**
   479	 * @defgroup ruby_imports Import Constructs
   480	 * @brief Ruby file loading mechanisms
   481	 *
   482	 * Ruby file loading:
   483	 * - `require 'lib'` - load from $LOAD_PATH (once)
   484	 * - `require_relative './file'` - load relative to current file
   485	 * - `load 'file.rb'` - always reload
   486	 * - `include Module` - mix in module methods as instance methods
   487	 * - `extend Module` - mix in module methods as class methods
   488	 * - `prepend Module` - insert module before class in method chain
   489	 * @{
   490	 */
   491	
   492	/// @brief Require - loads library from load path
   493	DEF_TYPE("require", EXTERNAL_IMPORT, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   494	
   495	/// @brief Require relative - loads file relative to current
   496	DEF_TYPE("require_relative", EXTERNAL_IMPORT, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   497	
   498	/// @brief Load - loads and re-evaluates file
   499	DEF_TYPE("load", EXTERNAL_IMPORT, NONE, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   500	
   501	/// @brief Include - mixes in module as instance methods
   502	DEF_TYPE("include", EXTERNAL_IMPORT, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   503	
   504	/// @brief Extend - mixes in module as class methods
   505	DEF_TYPE("extend", EXTERNAL_IMPORT, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   506	
   507	/// @brief Prepend - inserts module before class in method chain
   508	DEF_TYPE("prepend", EXTERNAL_IMPORT, FIND_IDENTIFIER, NONE, ASTNodeFlags::IS_KEYWORD)
   509	
   510	/** @} */ // end ruby_imports
   511	
   512	// ============================================================================
   513	// COMMENTS
   514	// ============================================================================
   515	
   516	/**
   517	 * @defgroup ruby_comments Comments
   518	 * @brief Documentation and annotation
   519	 * @{
   520	 */
   521	
   522	/// @brief Comment - `# single line comment`
   523	DEF_TYPE("comment", METADATA_COMMENT, NONE, NONE, 0)
   524	
   525	/** @} */ // end ruby_comments
   526	
   527	// ============================================================================
   528	// SPECIAL CONSTRUCTS
   529	// ============================================================================
   530	
   531	/**
   532	 * @defgroup ruby_special Special Ruby Constructs
   533	 * @brief Ruby-specific language features
   534	 *
   535	 * Ruby special constructs:

   413	/**

   415	 * @brief Ruby error handling constructs
   416	 *
   417	 * Ruby exception handling:

   420	 * - Multiple rescue clauses for different exception types
   421	 * - `retry` to re-execute begin block

   444	/**

   447	 *
   448	 * Ruby closures:
   449	 * - Blocks: `{ |params| body }` or `do |params| body end`
   450	 * - Procs: `Proc.new { }` or `proc { }`
   451	 * - Lambdas: `lambda { }` or `-> { }` (strict arity)
   452	 *
   453	 * Blocks are passed implicitly to methods:
   454	 * - `yield` calls the block
   455	 * - `block_given?` checks for block
   456	 * - `&block` captures block as Proc parameter
   457	 * @{
   458	 */

   976	/**
   977	 * @defgroup ruby_patterns Pattern Matching

   981	 * - `case expr in pattern ... end`
   982	 * - Variable binding: `in x` captures value
   983	 * - Array patterns: `in [a, b, *rest]`
   984	 * - Hash patterns: `in {name:, age:}`
   985	 * - Guard clauses: `in pattern if condition`
   986	 * @{
   987	 */

  1009

  1012	
  1013	/** @} */ // end ruby_patterns
